---
title: "Assemble plant species list for Barro Colorado Island, Panama"
author: "Paula Uzcategui and Helene Muller-Landau"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## Summary

This Rmarkdown merges information from multiple high-quality plant species lists for Barro Colorado Island (BCI), Panama, updates to the best current woody plant species names based on the current Smithsonian ForestGEO woody species lists, utilizes prior checks of species names against the World Checklist of Vascular Plants (WCVP) version 13, and outputs the combined high-quality list. The reason for using WCVP version 13 is because that is the version currently in use by PlantNet.  The contributing lists are Garwood et al. 2009, Schnitzer et al. 2024, Zotz 2024, the Smithsonian ForestGEO Panama woody plant species and ViewTaxonomy lists (Perez et al. 2025), and a file of tree species found in forest inventory plots on BCI.  These lists are considered high-quality because they were each assembled by one or more botanists familiar with the local flora, and build on the work of Croat 1978. Combining the lists involves checking overlap not only in the original names on each list, but also on two lists of "synonyms" for BCI plant species, as well as in the current accepted name.  The synonym lists include one from Garwood 2009 and two from Smithsonian ForestGEO.  In all cases, these include not only synonyms in the botanical sense, but also prior names used for the same BCI morphospecies (including in cases where species determination was revised).  

This Rmarkdown additionally outputs a list of plant species that appear in panamabiota.org's list of plants of Barro Colorado, that are *not* in the combined high-quality list. This list is generated for the purpose of later review.  

The component and output lists are all available in the public GitHub site https://github.com/PanamaForestGEO/Panama_plant_species_lists 


## References 

Croat, Thomas B. 1978. *Flora of Barro Colorado Island*. Stanford University Press.

Garwood, Nancy C., Nancy Hensold, and Robin B. Foster. 2009. Appendix 1.  Revised Species List for Barro Colorado Island, with Synonyms. In *Seedlings of Barro Colorado Island and the Neotropics*. Cornell University Press.

Pérez, Rolando, Salomón Aguilar and David Mitre.  2025. Lista de árboles, arbustos, palmas, lianas y enredaderas de la flora de Panamá.  Version 2025-12-04.  

Schnitzer, Stefan, David DeFilippis, Rigoberto Rivera-Camaña, et al. 2024. Liana Species of the Barro Colorado Island 50-Ha Plot 2007 and 2017 Censuses. Version 7. *Dryad* https://doi.org/10.5061/DRYAD.Q2BVQ83V4.

Zotz, Gerhard.  2024.  Table S1. Updated list of epiphyte species on BCI. Online Supplementary Material for Zotz, G. 2024. Epiphyte Research on BCI. In *The First 100 Years of Research on Barro Colorado: Plant and Ecosystem Science*, ed. H. C. Muller-Landau and S. J. Wright. Smithsonian Institution Scholarly Press. https://doi.org/10.5479/si.26880865

### To do 

When reading in files, it would be good to rename columns right away if necessary to get rid of blanks, /’s, and other problematic characters, so subsequent references can be simplified.

for kable(zotznocroatbutmatched %>% filter(firstmatchtype=="orig-syn"),
      caption="Zotz species that Zotz says do not appear in Croat 1978 that nonetheless
      matched to a Garwood synonym. ")
      
      change to giving the name or names that show up in Garwood for this taxon.  
      
    
## Setup

### Load libraries
```{r setup2,}

rm(list=ls())

library(readxl)
library(writexl)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringi)
library(stringr)
library(knitr)
library(DT)
```

### Read input files and initial data prep

```{r readprepdata, echo=TRUE, results="hide"}
# --- File paths ---
FILE_GARWOOD          <- "splists_out/Garwood_2009_Appendix1HM2_WCVP_2026-03-30.xlsx"
FILE_GARWOOD_SYN      <- "splists_raw/Garwood/Garwood_2009_Appendix1_Synonyms.xlsx"
FILE_ZOTZ             <- "splists_out/83_Zotz_Epiphytes_TableS1_WCVP_2026-03-25.xlsx"
FILE_LIANAS           <- "splists_out/Lianas_Species_complete_list_WCVP_2026-03-16.xlsx"
FILE_PBIOTA           <- "splists_out/PanamaBiotaBCI_WCVP_2026-02-26.xlsx"
FILE_PANWOODY         <- "splists_out/CurrentPanamaWoody_2026-03-26.xlsx"
FILE_PANWOODY_SYN     <- "splists_out/alternates_and_synonyms/CurrentPanamaWoody_SYNONYMS_2026-03-26.xlsx"
FILE_ALTNAMES         <- "splists_out/alternates_and_synonyms/AltNames_2026-03-31.xlsx"
FILE_ALTNAMES_GARWOOD <- "splists_out/alternates_and_synonyms/alternate_names_garwood_2026-03-31.xlsx"
FILE_WRIGHT           <- "splists_out/wright_nomenclature_R_20210224_WCVP_2026-03-26.xlsx"

# --- Load data ---

# Original Garwood list + WCVP v13 names
garwood_list_orig <- read_excel(FILE_GARWOOD, col_types = "text")

# Garwood synonyms
garwood_synonyms <- read_excel(FILE_GARWOOD_SYN)

# Original Zotz list + WCVP v13 names
zotz_list <- read_excel(FILE_ZOTZ, col_types = "text") %>%
  rename_with(~ gsub("[()]", "", gsub(" ", "_", .x))) %>%
  rename(wcvp_accepted_lifeform = wcvp_lifeform_description)

# Original lianas list + WCVP v13 names
lianas_list <- read_excel(FILE_LIANAS, col_types = "text") %>%
  mutate(sp6 = tolower(sp6)) %>%
  rename(wcvp_accepted_lifeform = wcvp_lifeform_description)

# Original Panama biota list + WCVP v13 names
pbiota_list <- read_excel(FILE_PBIOTA, col_types = "text")

# Current Panama woody species list + WCVP v13 names
# This list has the names used in ForestGEO in the column "orig_name"
panwoody_list <- read_excel(FILE_PANWOODY, col_types = "text")
panwoody_synonyms <- read_excel(FILE_PANWOODY_SYN, col_types = "text")

# Alternate names of species in Garwood and Panama woody
alternate_names <- read_excel(FILE_ALTNAMES)

# Alternate names of species in Garwood and Panama woody that are directly or indirectly in Garwood
alternate_names_garwood <- read_excel(FILE_ALTNAMES_GARWOOD)

# Wright list
allwrighttaxa <- read_excel(FILE_WRIGHT, na = c("", "NA"))
```

Adding some columns to the Garwood list
```{r prepare garwood}
garwood_synonyms <- garwood_synonyms %>% mutate(
  sp_name = trimws(paste0(Genus, " ", Species)),
  synonym_sp = trimws(paste0(SynonymGenusSingle, " ", SynonymSpeciesSingle)),
  synonym_binomial = paste0(SynonymGenusSingle, " ", SynonymSpeciesSingle)) 
# note that synonym_sp and synonym_binomial are identical; retaining for clarity of completeness

garwood_list <- garwood_list_orig %>%
  mutate(wcvp_accepted_binomial = 
           paste0(wcvp_accepted_name_genus, " ", wcvp_accepted_name_species),
         wcvp_matched_binomial = word(wcvp_matched_name,)) %>%
  rename(wcvp_accepted_lifeform = wcvp_lifeform_description)
```


alternate_names_garwood is a list of alternate names that match to garwood. It is generated in the alternate_species_names.Rmd to be used in this script.

```{r buildaltmatchlookup}
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Build a flat lookup table used downstream to resolve BCI plot species and
# lianas list entries that did not match Garwood directly.
# Both the preferred name and the alternate name are indexed as possible keys
# so that either form can be used to retrieve the Garwood match.
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

alt_match_lookup <- bind_rows(
  alternate_names_garwood %>%
    select(match_key = prefname, garwood_orig_match, garwood_synonym_match,
           firstmatchtype, alt_name = altname, pref_name = prefname),
  alternate_names_garwood %>%
    select(match_key = altname,  garwood_orig_match, garwood_synonym_match,
           firstmatchtype, alt_name = altname, pref_name = prefname)
) %>%
  filter(!is.na(match_key)) %>%
  distinct(match_key, .keep_all = TRUE)



```


### Output file names 

MAIN OUTPUTS: 

- Combined, high-quality list of BCI plant species.  

- List of BCI species that might be woody according to WCVP lifeform. 

ADDITIONAL OUTPUTS OF FILES TO CHECK: 

- List of Zotz species not in Garwood

- List of BCI plot species not in Garwood

- List of BCI liana list species not in Garwood

- List of PanamaBiota BCI species that are not in the combined high-quality list

- List of Wright species that are not in the Current Panama Woody list but that might be woody by the lifeform in Wright, WCVP and Garwood. 


```{r outfilenames}

current_date <- Sys.Date()

FILECOMBINEDLIST <- paste0("splists_out/Plantnet/BCI_SPECIES_BOTANISTS_LIST_",current_date,".xlsx")

FILEBCIWOODY <- paste0("splists_out/needs_revision/BCI_SPECIES_POSSIBLY_WOODY_",current_date,".xlsx")


FILECHECKZOTZ <- paste0("splists_out/needs_revision/zotz_species_not_in_garwood_", current_date, ".xlsx")

FILECHECKPLOTS <- paste0("splists_out/needs_revision/plotsbci_not_garwood_", current_date, ".xlsx")

FILECHECKLIANAS <- paste0("splists_out/needs_revision/lianasbci_not_garwood_", current_date, ".xlsx")

FILECHECKPANAMABIOTA <- paste0("splists_out/needs_revision/panamabiota_not_garwood_", current_date, ".xlsx")

FILECHECKWRIGHT <- paste0("splists_out/needs_revision/Wright_not_CurrentPanamaWoody_", current_date, ".xlsx")

```

## Assembling a combined BCI high-quality botanist-determined plant species list

```{r columnsetup}
# Initializing the columns that all of the species added should have
listscol <- c("orig_name",
              "orig_binomial",
              "wcvp_accepted_binomial",
              "wcvp_matched_name",
              "wcvp_matched_plant_name_id",
              "wcvp_matched_ipni_id",
              "wcvp_matched_status",
              "wcvp_accepted_name",
              "wcvp_accepted_family", 
              "wcvp_accepted_authority",
              "wcvp_accepted_plant_name_id", 
              "wcvp_accepted_powo_id",
              "wcvp_accepted_ipni_id",
              "wcvp_accepted_lifeform",
              "source_list"
              )

# Create the empty dataframe of BCI species list
bcilist <- data.frame(matrix(ncol = length(listscol), nrow = 0))
colnames(bcilist) <- listscol

```


### Garwood 2009 list

Some species in Garwood are listed as being only in the Barro Colorado Nature Monument but not on BCI itself (chg=="BCNM"). However, after matching with species recorded in the plots in BCI these species show up. Because of that we decided not to remove these species listed as only BCNM.

Some examples are: 

Matayba oppositifolia, Myrcia zetekiana, Buchenavia tetraphylla. 

Buchenavia tetraphylla is an alternate name of Terminalia tetraphylla which is found in the plot censuees of BCI.

```{r garwoodnobcnm}

yesonbcibinomial <- c("Matayba oppositifolia", "Myrcia zetekiana", "Buchenavia tetraphylla")

#garwood_list <- garwood_list %>% filter(chg != "BCNM" | is.na(chg) | #orig_name %in% yesonbcibinomial)
garwoodtoremove <- subset(garwood_list_orig, chg=="BCNM")

kable(garwoodtoremove %>% select(orig_name,SYNONYMS),
      caption = "Species that according to Garwood are in the mainland peninsulas but not on BCI itself.")

# these used to be removed, but not anymore. 


# remove garwood that is NA
# and leave out Ficus aff. Trigonata, it is already in zotz withputh the aff.
garwood_list <- garwood_list %>% filter(!is.na(orig_name)  & orig_name != "Ficus aff. trigonata")

```



```{r garwoodnoid}

# view unidentified species
garwoodnobinomial <- subset(garwood_list_orig,is.na(word(orig_binomial,2)))

kable(garwoodnobinomial %>% select(orig_name,Syn_notes) %>% rename(garwood_name=orig_name),
      caption="Garwood species lacking a complete binomial")

```

This leaves `r nrow(garwood_list)` plant species on the Garwood list.  Note that in `r nrow(garwoodnobinomial)` cases these species lack proper binomials, indicating their species identity remains uncertain (including possible new species).  These are left in anyway because they are known morphospecies.  


Add Garwood species to the BCI list. 
```{r startbcilist}

garwood_list$source_list = "Garwood2009"
garwood_list$in_garwood = TRUE
garwood_list$garwood_name = garwood_list$orig_name
garwood_list$garwood_synonyms = garwood_list$SYNONYMS

          
# Some additional columns in Garwood. 
bcilist <- garwood_list %>% select(all_of(c(listscol,"in_garwood","garwood_name", "garwood_synonyms", "LF_RF", "BCI cult", "chg")))

bcilist <- bcilist %>% mutate(
  bci_rowid = row_number()
)
```  

### Zotz 2024 epiphyte species list

*There are `r nrow(zotz_list)` species on the Zotz 2024 epiphyte list.  *

Next we match Zotz species to the Garwood list as far as possible, including by synonyms in the broad sense, and identify Zotz species thata are not on the Garwood list.  

Note that we make one manual correction to a Zotz misspelling of a Croat name to enable matching with the Garwood list for that name.  Other misspellings to the Zotz names themselves are addressed by the matching with WCVP.  

If a species is both in Garwood and Zotz, we will keep the Zotz names for the matches since they are the most up-to-date names. 

If the name in Garwood is in Zotz trough a synonym and the Zotz name does not have a croat name, we keep the name in Garwood...
```{r prepzotz}

listscol <- c(listscol, "zotz_name", "in_zotz")

bcilist$zotz_name = NA_character_
bcilist$in_zotz = FALSE

# removing morphospecies identified only at the genus level (there is only one)
zotz_list <- zotz_list %>% filter(is.na(orig_binomial) | !is.na(word(orig_binomial,2) ))

# setting column indicating zotz list presence
zotz_list$source_list = "Zotz"
zotz_list$in_zotz = TRUE
zotz_list$zotz_name = zotz_list$orig_name


# when the name listed is the same as in Croat 1978, the column Sp_Croat_1978 is blank (NA)
# so create a new name_Croat field that is the name of the species in Croat 1978
zotz_list <- zotz_list %>% 
  mutate(wcvp_accepted_binomial = 
           paste0(wcvp_accepted_name_genus," ",wcvp_accepted_name_species),
         wcvp_matched_binomial = word(wcvp_matched_name,1,2),
         croat_name = ifelse(!is.na(Sp_Croat_1978),Sp_Croat_1978,orig_name)) %>%
  mutate(croat_binomial = word(croat_name,1,2)) 

# make one manual correction so matches work right
zotz_list <- zotz_list %>%
  mutate(croat_binomial =ifelse(croat_binomial=="Bolbiltis nicotianifolia",
         "Bolbitis nicotianifolia",croat_binomial))
```



#### Case 1: the species is directly in Zotz with the same name as Zotz

```{r case1zotz}
zotz_list <- zotz_list %>% mutate(zotz_row_id = row_number())


bcilist <- bcilist %>% mutate(
  in_zotz = orig_name %in% zotz_list$orig_name,
  zotz_name = ifelse(in_zotz, orig_name, zotz_name)
)


garwooddirect_match <- bcilist %>% filter(in_zotz)

datatable(zotz_list %>% 
            filter(orig_name %in% bcilist$orig_name & (is.na(Sp_Croat_1978) | Sp_Croat_1978 == "**" )) %>% select(orig_name, Sp_Croat_1978), 
          caption="Zotz species that Zotz says do not appear in Croat 1978 that nonetheless matched in Garwood, and appear with the same name in both")

```

`r nrow(garwooddirect_match)` Garwood names matched directly to Zotz

#### Case 2. Garwood species that are in Zotz by the binomial
```{r case2zotz}

garwoodzotz_binomial <- bcilist %>% 
  select(garwood_name, orig_binomial,bci_rowid, in_zotz ) %>%
  filter(!in_zotz) %>%
  left_join(zotz_list %>% 
              select(all_of(listscol), croat_name = Sp_Croat_1978, zotz_row_id),
           by = "orig_binomial"
           ) %>% 
  mutate(zotz_name = orig_name) %>%
  filter(!is.na(zotz_name))%>%
  # Add counts for each identifier to detect duplicates
  add_count(garwood_name, name = "n_garwood") %>%
  add_count(zotz_name, name = "n_zotz")

# 1. See where one Garwood name points to multiple Zotz records
datatable(garwoodzotz_binomial %>%
            filter(n_garwood > 1) %>%
            select(garwood_name, garwood_binomial = orig_binomial, zotz_name, croat_name, n_garwood), 
          caption = "Garwood binomial matches to multiple Zotz names. This means Zotz makes a distinction between subspecies while Garwood does not.")

# 2. See where multiple Garwood records point to one Zotz name (Many-to-One)
datatable(garwoodzotz_binomial %>%
            filter(n_zotz > 1) %>%
            select(garwood_name, garwood_binomial = orig_binomial, zotz_name, croat_name, n_zotz), 
          caption = "Multiple Garwood records match to the same Zotz name. This means Garwood makes a distinction between subspecies whil Zotz does not. And needs revision")

# flag this two zotz name as needing revision since they have binomials in garwood
ids_to_revise <- garwoodzotz_binomial %>%
  filter(n_zotz > 1 | n_garwood > 1) %>%
  pull(zotz_row_id)

zotz_list <- zotz_list %>%
  mutate(needs_revision = ifelse(zotz_row_id %in% ids_to_revise, TRUE, FALSE),
         revision_notes = ifelse(zotz_row_id %in% ids_to_revise, "Binomial is in the Garwood list, but Garwood makes a distinction of two subspecies", "")
         )

# The Garwood names that will change: 
garwood_change_zotz <- garwoodzotz_binomial %>%
            filter(n_zotz == 1 & n_garwood == 1) 
  
datatable(garwood_change_zotz %>%
            select(garwood_name, garwood_binomial =orig_binomial, zotz_name, croat_name), 
          caption="Garwood binomial matches a binomial in Zotz.")

garwood_change_zotz <- garwood_change_zotz %>%
    # only change the name if it is not conflicting with another in the list
  filter(!zotz_name %in% bcilist$orig_name) %>%
  mutate(source_list = "Zotz", in_zotz = TRUE) %>%
  select(all_of(listscol), in_zotz, bci_rowid)

cols_update <- setdiff(colnames(garwood_change_zotz),"bci_rowid")

# Join and merge values column by column
bcilist <- bcilist %>%
  left_join(garwood_change_zotz, by = "bci_rowid", suffix = c("", "_new")) %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(cols_update), ~ coalesce(get(paste0(cur_column(), "_new")), .))) %>%
  # Clean up the temporary join columns
  select(-ends_with("_new"))

```

In this case, all of these species are in Zotz without a subspecies. Since Zotz is the most up-to-date. We will set the Zotz name as the current name. 

With Epiphyllum phyllanthus, Garwood makes an distinction between two subspecies while Zotz does not. In this case we leave the Garwood name. 

#### Case 3. Garwood species that match zotz via the Croat name

The Croat name is a name previously used to refer to the species. So if Garwood has the same name as the Croat name Zotz is using, it refers to the same species.

```{r case3zotz}

garwoodzotz_croat <- bcilist %>% 
  select(garwood_name, in_zotz, bci_rowid) %>%
  filter(!in_zotz & !is.na(garwood_name)) %>%
  left_join(zotz_list %>% filter(!is.na(Sp_Croat_1978)) %>% 
              select(all_of(listscol), 
                     croat_name = Sp_Croat_1978, zotz_row_id),
           by = c("garwood_name" = "croat_name")
           ) %>% 
  mutate(zotz_name = orig_name) %>%
  filter(!is.na(zotz_name)) %>%
  # Add counts for each identifier to detect duplicates
  add_count(garwood_name, name = "n_garwood") %>%
  add_count(zotz_name, name = "n_zotz") 

# 1. See where one Garwood name points to multiple Zotz records
datatable(garwoodzotz_croat %>%
            filter(n_garwood > 1) %>%
            select(garwood_name, zotz_name, n_garwood), 
          caption = "Garwood name matches to multiple Zotz records")

# 2. See where multiple Garwood records point to one Zotz name (Many-to-One)
datatable(garwoodzotz_croat %>%
            filter(n_zotz > 1) %>%
            select(garwood_name, zotz_name, n_zotz), 
          caption = "Multiple Garwood records match to the same Zotz name")


# The Garwood names that will change: 
garwood_change_zotz <- garwoodzotz_croat %>%
            filter(n_zotz == 1 & n_garwood == 1 ) 


# falg this as needing revision
ids_to_revise <- garwoodzotz_croat %>%
  filter(zotz_name %in% bcilist$orig_name) %>%
  pull(zotz_row_id)

zotz_list <- zotz_list %>%
  mutate(needs_revision = ifelse(zotz_row_id %in% ids_to_revise, TRUE, needs_revision),
         revision_notes = ifelse(zotz_row_id %in% ids_to_revise, paste("Croat name maps to two different species in Garwood", revision_notes), revision_notes)
         )

  
datatable(garwood_change_zotz %>%
            select(garwood_name, zotz_name), 
          caption="Garwood name is the same as a Croat name in Zotz")

garwood_change_zotz <- garwood_change_zotz %>%
  # only change the name if it is not conflicting with another in the list
  filter(!zotz_name %in% bcilist$orig_name) %>%
  mutate(source_list = "Zotz", in_zotz = TRUE) %>%
  select(all_of(listscol), in_zotz, bci_rowid)



cols_update <- setdiff(colnames(garwood_change_zotz),"bci_rowid")

# Join and merge values column by column
bcilist <- bcilist %>%
  left_join(garwood_change_zotz, by = "bci_rowid", suffix = c("", "_new")) %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(cols_update), ~ coalesce(get(paste0(cur_column(), "_new")), .))) %>%
  # Clean up the temporary join columns
  select(-ends_with("_new"))

```

In this case, we use the Zotz name.

#### Case 4. Garwood matched Zotz only via WCVP matched or accepted names. These are all cases with a different spelling in Zotz. We will keep the Zotz spelling

```{r case4zotz}

garwoodzotz_wcvp <- bcilist %>% 
  select(garwood_name, in_zotz, bci_rowid, 
         wcvp_matched_name) %>%
  filter(!in_zotz) %>%
  left_join(zotz_list %>% 
              select(all_of(listscol), 
                     croat_name = Sp_Croat_1978, zotz_row_id),
           by = "wcvp_matched_name"
           ) %>% 
  mutate(zotz_name = orig_name) %>%
  filter(!is.na(zotz_name))%>%
  # Add counts for each identifier to detect duplicates
  add_count(garwood_name, name = "n_garwood") %>%
  add_count(zotz_name, name = "n_zotz")

# 1. See where one Garwood name points to multiple Zotz records
datatable(garwoodzotz_wcvp %>%
            filter(n_garwood > 1) %>%
            select(garwood_name, zotz_name, wcvp_matched_name, n_garwood), 
          caption = "Garwood name matches to multiple Zotz records")

# 2. See where multiple Garwood records point to one Zotz name (Many-to-One)
datatable(garwoodzotz_wcvp %>%
            filter(n_zotz > 1) %>%
            select(garwood_name, zotz_name, wcvp_matched_name, n_zotz), 
          caption = "Multiple Garwood records match to the same Zotz name")

# The Garwood names that will change: 
garwood_change_zotz <- garwoodzotz_wcvp %>%
            filter(n_zotz == 1 & n_garwood == 1) 


datatable(garwood_change_zotz %>%
            select(garwood_name, zotz_name, wcvp_matched_name), 
          caption="Garwood and Zotz name matched with the WCVP matched code. These are all cases in which the name has a different spelling in Zotz")

garwood_change_zotz <- garwood_change_zotz %>%
  mutate(source_list = "Zotz", in_zotz = TRUE) %>%
  select(all_of(listscol), in_zotz, bci_rowid)

cols_update <- setdiff(colnames(garwood_change_zotz),"bci_rowid")

# Join and merge values column by column
bcilist <- bcilist %>%
  left_join(garwood_change_zotz, by = "bci_rowid", suffix = c("", "_new")) %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(cols_update), ~ coalesce(get(paste0(cur_column(), "_new")), .))) %>%
  # Clean up the temporary join columns
  select(-ends_with("_new"))

```

Matches by accepted names. These are all cases with a name change according to WCVP

```{r case4zotz_1}

garwoodzotz_wcvp <- bcilist %>% 
  select(garwood_name = orig_name, in_zotz, bci_rowid, 
         wcvp_accepted_name) %>%
  filter(!in_zotz) %>%
  left_join(zotz_list %>% 
              select(all_of(listscol), 
                     croat_name = Sp_Croat_1978, zotz_row_id),
           by = "wcvp_accepted_name"
           ) %>% 
  mutate(zotz_name = orig_name) %>%
  filter(!is.na(zotz_name))%>%
  # Add counts for each identifier to detect duplicates
  add_count(garwood_name, name = "n_garwood") %>%
  add_count(zotz_name, name = "n_zotz")

# 1. See where one Garwood name points to multiple Zotz records
datatable(garwoodzotz_wcvp %>%
            filter(n_garwood > 1) %>%
            select(garwood_name, zotz_name, wcvp_matched_name, n_garwood), 
          caption = "Garwood name matches to multiple Zotz records")

# 2. See where multiple Garwood records point to one Zotz name (Many-to-One)
datatable(garwoodzotz_wcvp %>%
            filter(n_zotz > 1) %>%
            select(garwood_name, zotz_name, wcvp_matched_name, n_zotz), 
          caption = "Multiple Garwood records match to the same Zotz name")


garwood_change_zotz <- garwoodzotz_wcvp %>%
            filter(n_zotz == 1 & n_garwood == 1) 


datatable(garwood_change_zotz %>%
            filter(garwood_name==wcvp_accepted_name) %>%
            select(garwood_name, zotz_name, wcvp_accepted_name), 
          caption="Garwood and Zotz name matched with WCVP accepted name. These are all cases in which the Garwood name is the accepted name according to WCVP")


datatable(garwood_change_zotz %>%
            filter(zotz_name!=wcvp_accepted_name & garwood_name!=wcvp_accepted_name) %>%
            select(garwood_name, zotz_name, wcvp_accepted_name), 
          caption="Garwood and Zotz name matched with WCVP accepted name. These are all cases in which both species point to the same WCVP accepted name, according to WCVP they are both synonyms.")

# adding to revision for the last two cases:
# falg this as needing revision
ids_to_revise <- garwood_change_zotz %>%
  filter(garwood_name==wcvp_accepted_name | 
        (zotz_name!=wcvp_accepted_name & garwood_name!=wcvp_accepted_name)) %>%
  pull(zotz_row_id)

zotz_list <- zotz_list %>%
  mutate(needs_revision = ifelse(zotz_row_id %in% ids_to_revise, TRUE, needs_revision),
         revision_notes = ifelse(zotz_row_id %in% ids_to_revise, paste("Zotz name may have changed according to WCVP and Garwood.", revision_notes), revision_notes)
         )


```


```{r garwood_is_synonym}

# The Garwood names that will change: 
datatable(garwood_change_zotz %>%
            filter(zotz_name==wcvp_accepted_name) %>%
            select(garwood_name, zotz_name, wcvp_accepted_name), 
          caption="Garwood and Zotz name matched with WCVP accepted name. These are all cases in which the Garwood name is a synonym.")

garwood_change_zotz <- garwood_change_zotz %>%
  filter(zotz_name==wcvp_accepted_name) %>%
  mutate(source_list = "Zotz", in_zotz = TRUE) %>%
  select(all_of(listscol), in_zotz, bci_rowid)

cols_update <- setdiff(colnames(garwood_change_zotz),"bci_rowid")

# Join and merge values column by column
bcilist <- bcilist %>%
  left_join(garwood_change_zotz, by = "bci_rowid", suffix = c("", "_new")) %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(cols_update), ~ coalesce(get(paste0(cur_column(), "_new")), .))) %>%
  # Clean up the temporary join columns
  select(-ends_with("_new"))

```

#### Case 5. Species in garwood that match Zotz by a synonym.

```{r case5zotz}

zotz_lookup_long <- zotz_list %>%
  # Select the row ID and all the columns you want to match against
  select(zotz_row_id, 
         orig_binomial, 
         croat_name = Sp_Croat_1978, 
         wcvp_matched_binomial, 
         wcvp_accepted_binomial) %>%
  # Pivot so all names are in one column
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = -zotz_row_id, 
    names_to = "matchtype", 
    values_to = "match_key"
  ) %>%
  # Clean up: remove NAs so you don't match on empty cells
  filter(!is.na(match_key)) %>%
  distinct(match_key, .keep_all = TRUE)

garwood_synonymzotz_all <- garwood_synonyms %>%
  select(garwood_name = sp_name, 
         garwood_synonym = synonym_sp,
         garwood_synonym_binomial = synonym_binomial) %>%
  # Single join against the long lookup
  left_join(zotz_lookup_long, by = c("garwood_synonym_binomial" = "match_key")) %>%
  filter(!is.na(zotz_row_id)) %>%
  # Now bring back the rest of the Zotz data using the ID
  left_join(
    zotz_list %>% select(zotz_row_id, croat_name = Sp_Croat_1978, all_of(listscol)),
    by = "zotz_row_id"
  )

garwood_zotz_matched <- bcilist %>% filter(in_zotz)

garwood_synonymzotz <- garwood_synonymzotz_all %>%
  filter(garwood_name %in% bcilist$orig_name)%>%
  distinct(garwood_name, zotz_name, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  # Duplicate detection
  add_count(garwood_name, name = "n_garwood") %>%
  add_count(zotz_name, name = "n_zotz") %>%
  mutate(previous_direct_match = garwood_name %in% garwood_zotz_matched$orig_name)

```

```{r case5_revision}

datatable(garwood_synonymzotz %>%
            filter(previous_direct_match & !zotz_name %in% bcilist$orig_name) %>%
              select(zotz_name,croat_name,
                     zotz_matched_wcvp = wcvp_matched_name,
                     zotz_accepted_wcvp = wcvp_accepted_name,
                     garwood_name, garwood_synonym,matchtype)%>%
            arrange(garwood_name),
caption="Another species in the Zotz list had a more direct match to this garwood name. Leaving this zotz species out of the final list",
options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10)
           )

# flag these zotz names as needing revision 
ids_to_revise <- garwood_synonymzotz %>%
  filter(previous_direct_match & !zotz_name %in% bcilist$orig_name) %>%
  pull(zotz_row_id)

zotz_list <- zotz_list %>%
  mutate(needs_revision = ifelse(zotz_row_id %in% ids_to_revise, TRUE, needs_revision),
         revision_notes = ifelse(zotz_row_id %in% ids_to_revise, 
                                 paste0(revision_notes," Matches to Garwood through a garwood synonym, but another species in Zotz matches more directly"),revision_notes
         ))


garwood_synonymzotz <- garwood_synonymzotz %>% filter(!previous_direct_match)

datatable(
garwood_synonymzotz %>% 
  filter(n_garwood > 1) %>%
  select(garwood_name, garwood_synonym, zotz_name,
         zotz_matched_wcvp = wcvp_matched_name,
         zotz_accepted_wcvp = wcvp_accepted_name,
         matchtype)%>%
  arrange(garwood_name),
caption="Garwood has synonyms that match more than one zotz name",
options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10)
)

# flag these zotz names as needing revision 
ids_to_revise <- garwood_synonymzotz %>%
  filter(n_garwood > 1) %>%
  pull(zotz_row_id)

zotz_list <- zotz_list %>%
  mutate(needs_revision = ifelse(zotz_row_id %in% ids_to_revise, TRUE, needs_revision),
         revision_notes = ifelse(zotz_row_id %in% ids_to_revise, 
                                 paste0(revision_notes, " Matches to Garwood through a garwood synonym to the same name as another species in Zotz"), revision_notes)
         )


datatable(
garwood_synonymzotz %>% 
  filter(n_zotz > 1) %>%
  select(garwood_name, garwood_synonym, zotz_name,
         croat_name,
         zotz_matched_wcvp = wcvp_matched_name,
         zotz_accepted_wcvp = wcvp_accepted_name,
         matchtype)%>%
  arrange(zotz_name),
caption="Two Garwood names match the same Zotz name",
options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10)
)


# flag these zotz names as needing revision 
ids_to_revise <- garwood_synonymzotz %>%
  filter(n_zotz > 1) %>%
  pull(zotz_row_id)

zotz_list <- zotz_list %>%
  mutate(needs_revision = ifelse(zotz_row_id %in% ids_to_revise, TRUE, needs_revision),
         revision_notes = ifelse(zotz_row_id %in% ids_to_revise, 
                                 paste0(revision_notes, " Matches to two Garwood names through a Garwood synonym"), revision_notes)
         )


```

Zotz names that matched only by synonym to Garwood:
```{r}
datatable(
garwood_synonymzotz %>% 
  filter(n_zotz == 1 & n_garwood == 1 & (croat_name == "**" | is.na(croat_name))) %>%
  select(garwood_name, garwood_synonym, zotz_name, croat_name, matchtype),
caption="Zotz species that matched Garwood only by synonym and don't have a Croat name.",
options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10)
)


datatable(
garwood_synonymzotz %>% 
  filter(n_zotz == 1 & n_garwood == 1) %>%
  select(garwood_name, garwood_synonym, zotz_name, croat_name, matchtype),
caption="Zotz species that matched Garwood only by synonym.",
options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10)
)

# The Garwood names that will change: 
garwood_change_zotz <- garwood_synonymzotz %>%
            filter(n_zotz == 1 & n_garwood == 1) 

garwood_change_zotz <- garwood_change_zotz %>% left_join(
                  bcilist %>% 
                  select(bci_rowid, orig_name),
                  by=c("garwood_name"="orig_name")) 

garwood_change_zotz <- garwood_change_zotz %>%
  mutate(source_list = "Zotz", in_zotz = TRUE,
         orig_name = zotz_name,
         ) %>%
  select(all_of(listscol), in_zotz, bci_rowid)

cols_update <- setdiff(colnames(garwood_change_zotz),"bci_rowid")

# Join and merge values column by column
bcilist <- bcilist %>%
  left_join(garwood_change_zotz, by = "bci_rowid", suffix = c("", "_new")) %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(cols_update), ~ coalesce(get(paste0(cur_column(), "_new")), .))) %>%
  # Clean up the temporary join columns
  select(-ends_with("_new"))

```

#### Summary of Zotz names that need revision: 
```{r summary of revision}

datatable(
zotz_list %>% filter(needs_revision) %>%
  select(orig_name, Sp_Croat_1978, revision_notes),
caption="Zotz names that do not appear in the final BCI list because they might already be under a different name",
options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10))


```


```{r grawoodlookup}

# These will be added to the list:
zotz_no_match <- zotz_list %>% filter(!orig_name %in% bcilist$orig_name & 
                       !needs_revision) %>%select(orig_name)

# Double check the species that are being added
# create a look-up-table

garwood_names_lookup <- bind_rows(
  # 1. Original Binomials
  garwood_list %>% 
    select(matchkey = orig_binomial, garwood_name = orig_name) %>% 
    mutate(match_type = "orig_binomial"),
  
  # 2. WCVP Matched Binomials
  garwood_list %>% 
    select(matchkey = wcvp_matched_binomial, garwood_name = orig_name) %>% 
    mutate(match_type = "wcvp_matched_binomial"),
  
  # 3. Original Names (Full strings)
  garwood_list %>% 
    select(matchkey = orig_name, garwood_name = orig_name) %>% 
    mutate(match_type = "orig_name"),
  
  # 4. WCVP Accepted Names
  garwood_list %>% 
    select(matchkey = wcvp_accepted_name, garwood_name = orig_name) %>% 
    mutate(match_type = "wcvp_accepted_name"),
  
  # 5. Synonyms (from the synonyms table)
  garwood_synonyms %>% 
    select(matchkey = synonym_binomial, garwood_name = sp_name) %>% 
    mutate(match_type = "synonym_binomial"),
  
  # 6. Synonym species (no author)
  garwood_synonyms %>% 
    select(matchkey = synonym_sp, garwood_name = sp_name) %>% 
    mutate(match_type = "synonym_sp")
) %>% 
  # Clean up: Remove any NAs in the matchkey and remove exact duplicates
  filter(!is.na(matchkey)) %>% 
  distinct(matchkey, garwood_name, .keep_all = TRUE)


zotz_no_garwood <- zotz_list %>%
  filter(
    !orig_name %in% garwood_names_lookup$matchkey &
    !orig_binomial %in% garwood_names_lookup$matchkey &
    !croat_binomial %in% garwood_names_lookup$matchkey &
    !wcvp_matched_name %in% garwood_names_lookup$matchkey &
    !wcvp_matched_binomial %in% garwood_names_lookup$matchkey &
    !wcvp_accepted_binomial %in% garwood_names_lookup$matchkey
  )


# Double check that we are counting all of the species in our matches
failures <- setdiff(zotz_no_match$orig_name, zotz_no_garwood$orig_name)

if (length(failures) > 0) {
  stop(paste("Validation failed! These names are probably in Garwood", 
             paste(head(failures, 5), collapse = ", ")))
} else {
  message("Check passed: Double checked the species added to the BCI list.")
}

datatable(zotz_no_garwood  %>% 
          select(zotz_name, wcvp_matched_name, wcvp_accepted_name, Sp_Croat_1978), 
          caption="Zotz species that don't match anything in Garwood.  Note that in all cases Sp_Croat_1978 has value **, indicating that Zotz 2024 states that the species does not appear in Croat 1978.",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10))



# Writing Zotz species that need to be checked
zozt_revision_cols <- c("Species", "Sp_Croat_1978", "Family", "Fam_Croat_1978", "Life_form", "Lf_Croat_1978", "Source_for_additions", "NewName", "NewAuthority", "DateChange", "Notes", "wcvp_matched_name", "wcvp_matched_authors", "wcvp_matched_rank", "wcvp_matched_status", "wcvp_matched_plant_name_id", "wcvp_matched_ipni_id", "match_similarity", "wcvp_accepted_plant_name_id", "wcvp_accepted_powo_id", "wcvp_accepted_ipni_id", "wcvp_accepted_name", "wcvp_accepted_name_genus", "wcvp_accepted_name_species", "wcvp_accepted_name_rank", "wcvp_accepted_family", "wcvp_accepted_name_infraspecific_rank", "wcvp_accepted_name_infraspecies", "wcvp_accepted_author", "wcvp_accepted_authority", "wcvp_accepted_lifeform", "wcvp_accepted_binomial", "wcvp_matched_binomial", "needs_revision", "revision_notes")

zotz_to_revise <- bind_rows(
  zotz_list %>% filter(needs_revision) %>% select(all_of(zozt_revision_cols)),
  zotz_no_garwood %>% select(all_of(zozt_revision_cols))
)

write_xlsx(zotz_to_revise, FILECHECKZOTZ)
```

Adding the species to the BCI list
```{r}

# Adding zotz species to the BCI list
zotz_no_garwood$source_list = "Zotz"
zotz_no_garwood$in_zotz = TRUE


# Some additional columns in Garwood. 
bcilist <- bind_rows(
  bcilist,
  zotz_no_garwood %>% select(all_of(listscol)))

bcilist <- bcilist %>% mutate(
  bci_rowid = row_number()
)

```


In total there are `r nrow(zotz_no_garwood)` species on the Zotz list not already on the Garwood list.  

---

Next we match species recorded in BCI in plot census and in Lianas lists. Many of these species are expected to be in the Forestgeo list, in that case the Forestgeo name is more up-to-date than the Garwood name. We will first detect the species that are definitely missing from the Garwood list and add them, and then we will find the Forestgeo names for all of the species in the final list. 

### List of tree species recorded in censuses of forest plots on BCI 

Check that all the tree species recorded from forest plots on BCI are also in the combined species list.  

```{r plotslist}
# Preparing plotlist

# 1. Read data
bci_plots_abund <- read_excel("splists_raw/Plots/abundance_plots_HM.xlsx")

bci_plots_abund <- bci_plots_abund %>% 
  select(sp, binomial, bci, `bci 10 ha plot`, P11, P14) %>%
  rename(
    sp6 = sp,
    census_name = binomial,
    bci50ha = bci,
    bci10ha = `bci 10 ha plot`,
  ) 

bci_other_plots <- read.delim(
  "splists_raw/Plots/doi_10_5061_dryad_1g1jwsvc3__v20260226/mainfile.txt"
)

# 2. Summarise other plots safely
bci_other_plots <- bci_other_plots %>%
  group_by(sp6, plot) %>%
  summarise(n_distinct_tag = n_distinct(tag), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = plot,
    values_from = n_distinct_tag,
    values_fill = 0
  ) %>%
  rename(
    bci10ha = `10ha`,
    bci25ha = `25ha`
  )


# 3. Join safely (keeps all census_list species)
census_list <- full_join(bci_plots_abund, bci_other_plots, by = "sp6") %>%
  mutate(bci10ha = coalesce(bci10ha.x, bci10ha.y)) %>%
  select(-bci10ha.x, -bci10ha.y)

# 5. Filter safely (handle NA just in case)
census_list <- census_list %>%
  mutate(across(c(bci50ha, bci10ha, P14, P11, Zetek, bci25ha, AVA, Drayton, Pearson), ~coalesce(., 0))) %>%
  filter(
      if_any(c(P11, P14, bci10ha, bci50ha, Zetek, bci25ha, AVA, Drayton, Pearson),
             ~ !is.na(.) & . > 0)
    )


# 6. Create census_plot column safely
census_list <- census_list %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(
    census_plot = paste(
      c(
        if (bci50ha > 0) "bci50ha",
        if (bci10ha > 0) "bci10ha",
        if(bci25ha > 0) "bci25ha",
        if (P14 > 0) "P14",
        if (P11 > 0) "P11",
        if(Zetek > 0) "Zetek",
        if(AVA > 0) "AVA",
        if(Drayton > 0) "Drayton",
        if(Pearson > 0) "Pearson"
      ),
      collapse = ", "
    )
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# the plots files don't have wcvp accepted name, merge them by 6-letter species code in the Panama woody species list
match21 <- match(census_list$sp6, panwoody_list$sp6)            
nomatchview <- census_list[is.na(match21),] %>% select(sp6, census_name) # Those that don't match are all unidentified

if (nrow(nomatchview)>0){
knitr::kable(nomatchview, caption="Warning: Species recorded in plots on BCI whose 6-letter codes do not have a match in the Panama ForestGEO woody plant species database.")

}else{
  print("All species in the plot have a match to a sp6 code in the current woody list.")
}

census_list <- census_list %>% left_join(
  panwoody_list, by="sp6"
) %>%
  rename(forestgeo_current_name = orig_name)%>% 
# leaving out species that dont have a name
  filter(!is.na(forestgeo_current_name))
  

# Some names in the plots do not match the names in forestgeo
datatable(
census_list %>% 
  filter(!is.na(census_name) & (census_name != forestgeo_current_name)) %>% 
  select(sp6, sp4, census_name, forestgeo_current_name = forestgeo_current_name),
caption="Sp6 codes in the plots match to forestgeo codes, but some of the species names used in the plots do not match the forestgeo name associated with that code. "
)

datatable(
census_list %>% 
  filter(is.na(census_name)) %>% 
  select(sp6, sp4, census_name, forestgeo_current_name = forestgeo_current_name),
caption="Sp6 codes in the plots match to forestgeo codes, but species name is missing in the plots list. "
)

# We will use the forestgeo current name to make all the comparisons with this list
census_list <- census_list %>% mutate(orig_name = forestgeo_current_name)

# remove the row for "unidentified", if present
census_list <- census_list %>% filter(sp6!="uniden")

bcidups <- census_list %>% filter(duplicated(sp6)) %>% select(sp6, census_name)

if (nrow(bcidups)>0)
knitr::kable(bcidups, caption="Warning: Some 6-letter species codes are repeated in the census_list data.frame.")

# create binomial first if not already
census_list$wcvp_accepted_binomial <- paste0(
  census_list$wcvp_accepted_genus, " ",
  census_list$wcvp_accepted_species
)


census_list <- census_list %>% 
  mutate(
    in_census = TRUE,
    source_list = "Plots_census",
    census_row_id = row_number(),
    forestgeo_current_binomial = orig_binomial,
  )


datatable(
census_list %>%
  select(sp6, sp4, census_name, forestgeo_current_name,),
caption="BCI plots species list",
options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10)
)
```


*In total there are `r nrow(census_list)` tree morphospecies recorded for forest plot censuses on BCI.*

Matching to original garwood names (direct match) or to synonyms or alternate names that match to garwood.


#### 1. Direct match to garwood names

```{r bciplotdirectmatch}
# 1. direct name/binomial match
bci_plots_garwood_name_match <- census_list %>%
  filter(
    forestgeo_current_name %in% bcilist$orig_name |

    (!is.na(wcvp_matched_name) &
       wcvp_matched_name %in% bcilist$wcvp_matched_name) |

    (!is.na(wcvp_accepted_name) &
       wcvp_accepted_name %in% bcilist$wcvp_accepted_name) |

    # match binomials
    (!is.na(orig_binomial) & (
        orig_binomial %in% bcilist$orig_binomial 
    )) |

    (!is.na(wcvp_accepted_binomial) & (
        wcvp_accepted_binomial %in% bcilist$wcvp_accepted_binomial 
    ))
  )

datatable(
  bci_plots_garwood_name_match %>% 
  select(sp6, forestgeo_current_name, wcvp_matched_name, wcvp_accepted_name), 
          caption="BCI plot species matching directly to Garwood names",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10))


```
          
*Matched `r nrow(bci_plots_garwood_name_match)` species in the plots directly to garwood.*

#### 2. Alternate name match 
```{r plotalternatematchtry}


# 2. everything that did not match directly to garwood, match to alternate names
bci_plots_garwood_synonym_match <- census_list %>% 
  filter(
      !orig_name %in% bci_plots_garwood_name_match$orig_name &
    (
      orig_name %in% alt_match_lookup$match_key |
      orig_binomial %in% alt_match_lookup$match_key |
      
      (!is.na(wcvp_matched_name) &
         wcvp_matched_name %in% alt_match_lookup$match_key) |
        
      (!is.na(wcvp_accepted_name) &
         wcvp_accepted_name %in% alt_match_lookup$match_key)
    )
  )


# 3. print the altname it matched to and why that name is in garwood

bci_plots_garwood_synonym_print <- bci_plots_garwood_synonym_match %>%
  # Join using a chain of coalesce to check the most reliable fields first
  left_join(alt_match_lookup, by = c("orig_name" = "match_key")) %>%
  left_join(alt_match_lookup, by = c("orig_binomial" = "match_key"), suffix = c("", "_bi")) %>%
  left_join(alt_match_lookup, by = c("wcvp_matched_name" = "match_key"), suffix = c("", "_match")) %>%
  left_join(alt_match_lookup, by = c("wcvp_accepted_name" = "match_key"), suffix = c("", "_acc")) %>%
  
  # Now, condense everything into final fields
  mutate(
    orig_garwood    = coalesce(garwood_orig_match, garwood_orig_match_bi, garwood_orig_match_match, garwood_orig_match_acc),
    orig_garwood_synonym = coalesce(garwood_synonym_match, garwood_synonym_match_bi, garwood_synonym_match_match, garwood_synonym_match_acc),
    firstmatchtype  = coalesce(firstmatchtype, firstmatchtype_bi, firstmatchtype_match, firstmatchtype_acc),
    alternate_name  = coalesce(alt_name, alt_name_bi, alt_name_match, alt_name_acc),
    prefered_name   = coalesce(pref_name, pref_name_bi, pref_name_match, pref_name_acc)
  ) %>%
  select(-ends_with("_bi"), -ends_with("_match"), -ends_with("_acc")) # Clean up extra columns

```

*Matched `r nrow(bci_plots_garwood_synonym_match)` species in the plots by synonyms or alternate names to garwood.*

The table below shows BCI plot species that could not be matched directly to Garwood (2009) but were linked through an alternate name. The `firstmatchtype` column indicates which name pairing produced the link:

Definitions:

- F=ForestGEO

- G=Garwood

- Fcur = ForestGEO current name from most recent list

- Falt = ForestGEO alternate name from most recent list for Panama woody plants in general

- Gorig = Garwood 2009 original name

- Galt = Garwood 2009 alternate name

- **direct synonym** — the name appears directly in the Garwood list as a synonym

 Definitions:

F=ForestGEO

G=Garwood

Fcur = ForestGEO current name from most recent list

Falt = ForestGEO alternate name from most recent list for Panama woody plants in general

Gorig = Garwood 2009 original name

Galt = Garwood 2009 alternate name

 
- **Fcur-Gorig** — the current ForestGEO name matches a name listed by Garwood

- **Fcur-Gorigbi** — the current ForestGEO name matches the binomial of a Garwood original name.

- **Falt-Gorig** — a ForestGEO alternate name matches a name originally listed by Garwood

- **Falt-Gorigbi** — a ForestGEO alternate name matches the binomial of a Garwood original name.

- **Fcur-Galtbi** — the current ForestGEO name matches the binomial of a Garwood synonym

- **Falt-Galt** — a historical ForestGEO alternate name matches a synonym documented by Garwood

- **Falt-Galtbi** — a historical ForestGEO alternate name matches the binomial of a Garwood synonym

 

Note that "Garwood original name" refers to the name as it appears in the Garwood (2009) revision, not necessarily the currently accepted name.

```{r printaltmatches}
datatable(
  bci_plots_garwood_synonym_print %>% 
  select(sp6, forestgeo_current_name, wcvp_matched_name, wcvp_accepted_name, firstmatchtype, orig_garwood,
         alternate_name, prefered_name, orig_garwood_synonym), 
          caption="BCI plot species where an alternate name of the species matched to garwood",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10))


```

Printing species in the plots that are not in Garwood. 
```{r allnomatchbciplot}

# combine direct matches and matches by alternate names or synonyms
bci_plots_garwood_match <- bind_rows(
  bci_plots_garwood_name_match,
  bci_plots_garwood_synonym_match
) %>%
  distinct(sp6, .keep_all = TRUE)

# filter unmatched rows
bci_plots_no_garwood_at_all <- census_list %>%
  filter(!sp6 %in% bci_plots_garwood_match$sp6)

kable(bci_plots_no_garwood_at_all  %>% select(sp6, census_name, forestgeo_current_name , wcvp_matched_name, 
                                              wcvp_accepted_name, census_plot), 
          caption="Species recorded in BCI plots census that don't match anything in Garwood")



write_xlsx(bci_plots_no_garwood_at_all, FILECHECKPLOTS)

```

Added `r nrow(bci_plots_no_garwood_at_all)` completely new rows to the BCI list


```{r addplottobci}

# Remove "zotz_name" and "in_zotz" from listscol
listscol <- setdiff(listscol, c("zotz_name", "in_zotz"))

bcilist$forestgeo_current_name = NA_character_


bcilist <- bind_rows(
  bcilist,
  bci_plots_no_garwood_at_all %>% 
    mutate(source_list="Plot_census",
           orig_name = forestgeo_current_name # the orig_name in bcilist represents the name originally listed in the source list.
           ) %>%
    select(all_of(c(listscol, "census_name", "in_census", "census_plot", "bci50ha", "bci10ha", "P11", "P14", "Zetek", "Pearson", "Drayton", "bci25ha", "sp6", "sp4", "forestgeo_current_name"))
))


```


### Lianas BCI list

Check whether they are in Garwood directly or by alternate names.  
```{r lianasnobcilist}

lianas_list <- lianas_list %>% filter(isbci=="TRUE")
lianas_list <- lianas_list %>% distinct(orig_name, .keep_all = TRUE)
lianas_list <- lianas_list %>% 
  mutate(wcvp_accepted_binomial = paste(wcvp_accepted_name_genus, wcvp_accepted_name_species),
         former_binomial = ifelse(is.na(GenusFormer),NA,paste(GenusFormer,SpeciesOnlyFormer))) %>%
  mutate(former_name = paste(ifelse(is.na(SubspeciesFormer),former_binomial,
                                    paste(former_binomial,SubspeciesFormer))))
```

Working with `r nrow(lianas_list)` liana species. 

```{r lianasdirect}

# 1. direct name/binomial match
lianas_garwood_name_match <- lianas_list %>%
  filter(

    orig_name %in% bcilist$orig_name |

    (!is.na(wcvp_matched_name) &
       wcvp_matched_name %in% bcilist$wcvp_matched_name) |

    (!is.na(wcvp_accepted_name) &
       wcvp_accepted_name %in% bcilist$wcvp_accepted_name) |

    (!is.na(orig_binomial) & (
        orig_binomial %in% bcilist$orig_binomial 
    )) |

    (!is.na(wcvp_accepted_binomial) & (
        wcvp_accepted_binomial %in% bcilist$wcvp_accepted_binomial 
    ))
  )


#2 match of former liana name to current Garwood name 
lianas_formername_match <- lianas_list %>%
  filter(
    !orig_name %in% c(lianas_garwood_name_match$orig_name)
                      &
    (former_name %in% bcilist$orig_name | 
       former_binomial %in% bcilist$orig_binomial)  )

kable(lianas_formername_match %>% select(liana_former_name = former_name, lianas_name = orig_name),
      caption="Liana species in which former names matched to Garwood original name.")

```

Matched `r nrow(lianas_garwood_name_match) + nrow(lianas_formername_match)` species in the lianas list that are directly in Garwood. 

```{r lianaaltmatch}

#4 match of former liana name to Garwood synonym
lianas_formertosynonym_match <- lianas_list %>%
  filter(
    !orig_name %in% c(lianas_garwood_name_match$orig_name)&
    (former_name %in% garwood_synonyms$synonym_sp |
       former_binomial %in% garwood_synonyms$synonym_binomial))

# see species that matched by a synonym 
lianas_formertosynonym_print <- lianas_formertosynonym_match %>%  left_join(
    garwood_synonyms %>% select(garwood_name = sp_name, synonym_sp, syn_text = SynonymTextALL) %>% distinct(),
    by = c("former_name" = "synonym_sp")
  ) 

kable(lianas_formertosynonym_print %>% 
            select(liana_name = orig_name, liana_former_name = former_name, garwood_name, 
                   liana_wcvp_accepted_name =wcvp_accepted_name,
                   garwood_synonym = syn_text), 
          caption="Liana species whose former names matched a Garwood synonym.",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10))

# 5. everything that did not match directly to garwood, match to alternate names
lianas_garwood_alternate_match <- lianas_list %>% 
  filter(
       !orig_name %in% c(lianas_garwood_name_match$orig_name, lianas_formertosynonym_match$orig_name) &
    (
      orig_name %in% alt_match_lookup$match_key |
      orig_binomial %in% alt_match_lookup$match_key |
      
      (!is.na(wcvp_matched_name) &
         wcvp_matched_name %in% alt_match_lookup$match_key) |
        
      (!is.na(wcvp_accepted_name) &
         wcvp_accepted_name %in% alt_match_lookup$match_key)
    )
  )


# print the altname it matched to and why that name is in garwood

lianas_garwood_alternate_print <- lianas_garwood_alternate_match %>%
  # Join using a chain of coalesce to check the most reliable fields first
  left_join(alt_match_lookup, by = c("orig_name" = "match_key")) %>%
  left_join(alt_match_lookup, by = c("orig_binomial" = "match_key"), suffix = c("", "_bi")) %>%
  left_join(alt_match_lookup, by = c("wcvp_matched_name" = "match_key"), suffix = c("", "_match")) %>%
  left_join(alt_match_lookup, by = c("wcvp_accepted_name" = "match_key"), suffix = c("", "_acc")) %>%
  
  # Now, condense everything into your final fields
  mutate(
    orig_garwood    = coalesce(garwood_orig_match, garwood_orig_match_bi, garwood_orig_match_match, garwood_orig_match_acc),
    orig_garwood_synonym = coalesce(garwood_synonym_match, garwood_synonym_match_bi, garwood_synonym_match_match, garwood_synonym_match_acc),
    firstmatchtype  = coalesce(firstmatchtype, firstmatchtype_bi, firstmatchtype_match, firstmatchtype_acc),
    alternate_name  = coalesce(alt_name, alt_name_bi, alt_name_match, alt_name_acc),
    prefered_name   = coalesce(pref_name, pref_name_bi, pref_name_match, pref_name_acc)
  ) %>%
  select(-ends_with("_bi"), -ends_with("_match"), -ends_with("_acc")) # Clean up extra columns

```

The table below shows liana species that could not be matched directly to Garwood (2009) but were linked through an alternate name. The `firstmatchtype` column indicates which name pairing produced the link:


Note that "Garwood name" refers to the name as it appears in the Garwood (2009) revision, not necessarily the currently accepted name.

```{r showlianaalternate}
datatable(
  lianas_garwood_alternate_print %>% 
  select(liana_name = orig_name, garwood_name = orig_garwood, garwood_synonym = orig_garwood_synonym), 
          caption="Liana species that matched to garwood by an alternate name",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10))
```


Add the lianas name and in_liana values to the BCI list for the species that matched
```{r addlianas}

lianas_garwood_match <- bind_rows(
  lianas_garwood_name_match,
  lianas_formername_match,
  lianas_formertosynonym_match,
  lianas_garwood_alternate_match
) %>%
  distinct(orig_name, .keep_all = TRUE)

bcilist$lianas_name <- NA_character_
bcilist$in_lianas <- FALSE

# Update in_lianas and lianas_name for species already in bcilist that matched by orig_name
bcilist <- bcilist %>%
  rows_update(
    lianas_garwood_name_match %>%
      select(orig_name) %>%
      mutate(in_lianas = TRUE,
             lianas_name =  orig_name),
    by = "orig_name",
    unmatched = "ignore"
  )

# 1. Direct matches — orig_name exists in bcilist, just update the columns
bcilist <- bcilist %>%
  rows_update(
      lianas_formername_match %>%  # these also have orig_name matching bcilist
      select(lianas_name= orig_name, orig_name = former_name) %>%
      mutate(in_lianas = TRUE),
    by = "orig_name",
    unmatched = "ignore"
  )

# 2. Former-name-to-synonym matches — bcilist row is under orig_garwood name
bcilist <- bcilist %>%
  rows_update(
    lianas_formertosynonym_print %>%
      distinct(lianas_name = orig_name, orig_name = garwood_name) %>%
      mutate(in_lianas = TRUE),
    by = "orig_name"
  )

# 3. Alternate name matches — bcilist row is under orig_garwood
bcilist <- bcilist %>%
  rows_update(
    lianas_garwood_alternate_print %>%
      distinct(lianas_name = orig_name, orig_garwood ) %>%
      transmute(orig_name = orig_garwood, in_lianas = TRUE),
    by = "orig_name"
  )


```


Printing liana species that are not in the list so far and need to be added: 
```{r lianasnomatch}

lianas_no_garwood_at_all <- lianas_list %>%
  filter(!orig_name %in% lianas_garwood_match$orig_name)


# 6. Output and Display
kable(lianas_no_garwood_at_all %>%
  select(liana_former_name =former_name, 
         liana_name = orig_name, 
         liana_wcvp_matched_name = wcvp_matched_name,
         liana_wcvp_accepted_name = wcvp_accepted_name), 
          caption = "Lianas species recorded in the 50-ha plot that don't have a match in Garwood",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10))

write_xlsx(lianas_no_garwood_at_all, FILECHECKLIANAS)

```

Add to BCI list
```{r lianasbciadd}
listscol <- setdiff(listscol, c("in_census", "forestgeo_current_name", "census_plot", "source_current_name", "census_name"))

lianas_no_garwood_at_all$source_list = "lianas"
lianas_no_garwood_at_all$in_lianas = TRUE
lianas_no_garwood_at_all$lianas_name = lianas_no_garwood_at_all$orig_name

bcilist <- bind_rows(
  bcilist,
  lianas_no_garwood_at_all %>% select(all_of(listscol), "in_lianas", "lianas_name")
)


```

## Check consistency 
```{r savebotanists}

# Check consistency
# removing rows that are not identified at species level
kable(bcilist %>% filter(is.na(word(orig_binomial, 2))) %>% select(orig_name, wcvp_accepted_name), caption="Species to be removed from final list because they are not fully identified" )

bcilist <- bcilist %>% filter(!is.na(word(orig_binomial, 2))) 

bcidupnames <- bcilist %>% 
  filter(!is.na(orig_name)) %>%
  group_by(orig_name) %>% filter(n()> 1) 

if (nrow(bcidupnames)>0) {
  datatable(bcidupnames%>%
          select(orig_name, wcvp_accepted_name) %>% arrange(orig_name),
          caption="Species with duplicated original name",
  options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10)
          )
  
}

bcidupwcvp <- bcilist %>% group_by(wcvp_accepted_name) %>% 
            filter(n()> 1)

  
datatable(
  bcidupwcvp %>% 
    filter(orig_binomial != wcvp_accepted_binomial) %>%
    select(orig_name, wcvp_accepted_name)%>%
    arrange(wcvp_accepted_name), 
  caption="Species duplicated wcvp_accepted name. WCVP binomial changed",
  options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10)
          )
  
  
datatable(
  bcidupwcvp %>% 
    filter(orig_binomial == wcvp_accepted_binomial) %>%
    select(orig_name, wcvp_accepted_name)%>%
    arrange(wcvp_accepted_name), 
  caption="Species duplicated wcvp_accepted name. WCVP subspecies differs",
  options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10)
          )

datatable(
  bcidupwcvp %>% 
    filter(is.na(wcvp_accepted_name)) %>%
    select(orig_name, wcvp_accepted_name)%>%
    arrange(wcvp_accepted_name), 
  caption="No accepted name found",
  options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10)
          )
  

bcilist %>%
  count(source_list) %>%
  arrange(desc(n)) %>%
  kable(col.names = c("source_list", "count"),caption="Sources of morphospecies in the BCI plant species list.")

datatable(
  bcilist %>%
    select(wcvp_accepted_name, orig_name, source_list) %>% 
    arrange(source_list),
    caption="Species list for BCI, with the source of each entry",
  options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10))

```


# Use the Panama woody plant morphospecies list to link in current ForestGEO names and 6-letter and 4-letter species codes for all species.

The forestgeo name is the most up-to-date name for these species, as they are the names used by the botanists in Panama. Next we check if the names in the current panama woody list are different from the names in Garwood, in that case the name in forestgeo is preferred. We add a new columns "current name" abd  "current_name_source" with the preferred name and whether it is Garwood, Forestgeo, Zots or the Lianas list. 


```{r renamingsome}
#checkpoint...
bcilist_orig <- bcilist
```


```{r addforestgeocurrentname}
bcilist <- bcilist_orig
# For all the species in the bcilist
# check if they are directly on the forestgeo /woody list
# check if they have an alternate name on that list

# Add a column with the forestgeo name
# Flag when the forestgeo_current_name != orig_garwood name

# 1. Create lookup table for woody species
forestgeo_names_lookup <- bind_rows(
  panwoody_list %>% mutate(matchby = "Fcurr") %>% select(match_key = orig_name, forestgeo_current_name = orig_name, sp6, sp4, wcvp_accepted_duplicated, matchby),
  panwoody_list %>% mutate(matchby = "wcvpmatch") %>% select(match_key = wcvp_matched_name, forestgeo_current_name = orig_name, sp6, sp4, wcvp_accepted_duplicated, matchby),
    panwoody_list %>% mutate(matchby = "wcvpaccepted") %>% select(match_key = wcvp_accepted_name, forestgeo_current_name = orig_name, sp6, sp4, wcvp_accepted_duplicated, matchby),
) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(match_key)) %>%
  # Group and collapse to keep provenance if a key matched in multiple ways
  group_by(match_key, forestgeo_current_name, sp6, sp4, wcvp_accepted_duplicated) %>%
  summarise(
    matchby = paste(matchby, collapse = ", "),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

forestgeo_names_lookup <- forestgeo_names_lookup %>% mutate(row_id = row_number())

### CHECKS ###
# check if one match_key points to different forestgeo_current_name
# This happens because the wcvp accepted name is different from the forestgeo_current_name. 
# The botanist are confident these species are different. But to wcvp v13 they are the same. 
duplicate_matchkey1 <- forestgeo_names_lookup %>%
  group_by(match_key) %>%
  filter(n_distinct(forestgeo_current_name) > 1 ) %>%
  arrange(match_key)

#datatable(duplicate_matchkey1,
#          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10),
#  caption="The same key maps to a different current name. For this cases the current #forestgeo name is different from the wcvp accepted name. And the current forestgeo name is #believed to be the accepted name.")

# In this case we remove the species were the match_key is different from the forestgeo_current_name.
alt_names_remove <- duplicate_matchkey1 %>% filter(forestgeo_current_name != match_key)

forestgeo_names_lookup <- forestgeo_names_lookup %>% filter(!row_id %in% alt_names_remove$row_id)

panwoody_synonyms <- panwoody_synonyms %>% select(sp6,
  forestgeo_current_name = orig_name, altname=SynonymTextSingle
)


```

```{r foresgeoadddirect}

# Prepare for the matches
bcilist <- bcilist %>% mutate(row_id = row_number(),
                           current_name = NA_character_, 
                            source_current_name = NA_character_
                              )

```  

### Case 0. The species comes from a forestgeo list

```{r case0matchfg}
# 0. The source of the names is the forestgeo list
case0_fgsource <- bcilist %>% filter(!is.na(sp6)) %>% 
                  mutate(current_name = forestgeo_current_name,
                         source_current_name = "ForestGeo"
                         )

# The species that are uniquely in the plots and not in the garwood list already have their forestgeo name. 
datatable(case0_fgsource %>%
            select(sp6,sp4, orig_name, forestgeo_current_name, source_list, current_name, source_current_name),
          caption="Species that are only in the plot list already have a foresgeo code",
           options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10),
          )


bcilist_fgeomatch <- bcilist %>% filter(!orig_name %in% case0_fgsource$orig_name) %>% select(-sp6, -forestgeo_current_name)


matched_species <- case0_fgsource
```

### Case 1. The species name is a current forestgeoname

```{r case1fgname}

case1_directmatch <- bcilist_fgeomatch %>% 
  left_join(forestgeo_names_lookup, by = c("orig_name"= "match_key"), suffix = c("", "_orig")) %>%
  
  left_join(forestgeo_names_lookup, by = c("wcvp_matched_name"= "match_key"), suffix = c("", "_wcvpmatch")) 



case1_directmatch <- case1_directmatch %>%  mutate(
    forestgeo_current_name =     coalesce(
      forestgeo_current_name,
      forestgeo_current_name_wcvpmatch,
    ),
    sp6 = coalesce(
      sp6,
      sp6_wcvpmatch,
    ),
    sp4 = coalesce(
      sp4,
      sp4_wcvpmatch
    )) %>%   select(-ends_with(c("_orig", "_wcvpmatch"))) 

  
case1_directmatch <- case1_directmatch %>%
  mutate(
         current_name = forestgeo_current_name,
         source_current_name = "ForestGeo"
         )  %>%
  filter(!is.na(sp6) & 
           !orig_name %in% matched_species$orig_name &
           !is.na(current_name) & !current_name %in% matched_species$current_name) 

case1_directmatch %>% group_by(forestgeo_current_name) %>% filter(n() > 1)

datatable(case1_directmatch %>%
          select(sp6, orig_name, wcvp_matched_name, wcvp_accepted_name, forestgeo_current_name, source_list, matchby),
          caption="Species original name is a forestgeo_current_name",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10),
          )



matched_species <- bind_rows(case0_fgsource, case1_directmatch)

bcilist_fgeomatch <- bcilist %>% filter(!orig_name %in% matched_species$orig_name) %>% select(-sp6)

```


### Case 2. The species original name matches to forestgeo alternate name

```{r case2_1}


case2_forestgeoalternate <- bcilist_fgeomatch %>% 
  left_join(alternate_names %>% filter(in_panwoodycurr | in_viewtaxcurr) %>% select(altname, forestgeo_current_name= prefname , sp6), 
            by=c("orig_name" = "altname"),suffix = c("", "_orig")) %>%
  left_join(alternate_names %>% filter(in_panwoodycurr | in_viewtaxcurr) %>% select(altname, forestgeo_current_name = prefname , sp6), 
            by=c("wcvp_matched_name" = "altname"),suffix = c("", "_wcvpmatch")) 



```



```{r case2fgname}
datatable(case2_forestgeoalternate %>%
            filter(!is.na(forestgeo_current_name)
                   ) %>%
          select(sp6, orig_name,forestgeo_current_name,forestgeo_current_name_wcvpmatch, source_list),
          caption="Species original name is an alternate name and maps to two forestgeo names",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10),
          )

case2_forestgeoalternate <- case2_forestgeoalternate %>%  mutate(
    forestgeo_current_name =     coalesce(
      forestgeo_current_name,
      forestgeo_current_name_wcvpmatch
    ),
    sp6 = coalesce(
      sp6,
      sp6_wcvpmatch
    )) %>%   select(-ends_with(c("_orig",  "_prefmatch", "_wcvpmatch"))) 



case2_forestgeoalternate <- case2_forestgeoalternate %>%
  mutate(
         current_name = forestgeo_current_name,
         source_current_name = "ForestGeo"
         )%>%
  filter(!is.na(sp6) & 
           !orig_name %in% matched_species$orig_name &
           !is.na(current_name) & !current_name %in% matched_species$current_name
         )


datatable(case2_forestgeoalternate %>%
          select(sp6, orig_name,forestgeo_current_name, source_list),
          caption="Species original name is a forestgeo alternate name",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10),
          )


matched_species <- bind_rows(case0_fgsource, case1_directmatch, case2_forestgeoalternate)

bcilist_fgeomatch <- bcilist %>% filter(!orig_name %in% matched_species$orig_name) %>% select(-sp6)

```



### Case 3. The species alternate name matches to one forestgeo current name


```{r case3fgname}

# Here we have to be careful, a species can have multiple alt names...
case3_forestgeoalternate <- bcilist_fgeomatch %>% 
  left_join(alternate_names %>% 
              #filter(!in_panwoodycurr & !in_viewtaxcurr)  %>%
              select(altname, prefname), 
            by=c("orig_name" = "prefname"), relationship = "many-to-many", suffix=c("","_orig")) %>%
  left_join(alternate_names %>% 
              #filter(!in_panwoodycurr & !in_viewtaxcurr)  
            select(altname, prefname), 
            by=c("wcvp_matched_name" = "prefname"), relationship = "many-to-many", suffix=c("","_wcvpmatch"))


case3_forestgeoalternate <- case3_forestgeoalternate %>% 
  mutate(
    altname = coalesce(
      altname,
      altname_wcvpmatch
    )) %>%   select(-ends_with(c("_orig", "_wcvpmatch")))

# Keep only the ones that have alternate names
case3_forestgeoalternate <- case3_forestgeoalternate %>% filter(!is.na(altname)) %>%
  distinct(orig_name, altname, .keep_all = TRUE) # clearing duplicates


# Find the forestgeo name that matches to that alternate name
case3_forestgeoalternate <- case3_forestgeoalternate %>%
  left_join(panwoody_list %>% select(forestgeo_current_name = orig_name, sp6), by = c("altname" = "forestgeo_current_name"))


datatable(case3_forestgeoalternate %>%
            filter(!is.na(sp6)) %>%
            group_by(orig_name) %>% 
            filter(n() > 1) %>%
            select(sp6, orig_name, altname,source_list),
          caption="Two or more alternate names for the species match a current forestgeo name.")

```


In this case,  Zanthoxylum kellermanii is in Garwood and not in Forestgeo. However Garwood notes it has two alternate names: Zanthoxylum panamense and Zanthoxylum riedelianum, both are in Forestgeo. Zanthoxylum panamense is already in the list and Zanthoxylum ridelianum gets matched by the WCVP accepted binomial later in this script.


```{r case3_2}

datatable(case3_forestgeoalternate %>%
            filter(!is.na(sp6)) %>%
            group_by(orig_name) %>% 
            filter(n() == 1) %>%
            select(sp6, orig_name, altname, source_list),
          caption="Alternate names for the species match a current forestgeo name.")

case3_forestgeoalternate <- case3_forestgeoalternate %>%
        filter(!is.na(sp6)) %>%
        group_by(orig_name) %>% 
        filter(n() == 1) %>%
        ungroup() %>%
  mutate(current_name = altname,
         source_current_name = "ForestGeo",
         forestgeo_current_name = altname
         ) %>%   
  filter(!orig_name %in% matched_species$orig_name &
           !is.na(current_name) & !current_name %in% matched_species$current_name
         )

matched_species <- bind_rows(case0_fgsource, case1_directmatch, case2_forestgeoalternate, case3_forestgeoalternate)

bcilist_fgeomatch <- bcilist %>% filter(!orig_name %in% matched_species$orig_name) %>% select(-sp6, -forestgeo_current_name)


```



### Case 4. The species alternate name matches to  one forestgeo alternate name
```{r case4fgname}

# Here we have to be careful, a species can have multiple alt names...
case4_forestgeoalternate <- bcilist_fgeomatch %>% 
  left_join(alternate_names %>% 
              #filter(!in_panwoodycurr & !in_viewtaxcurr)  
             select(altname, prefname), 
            by=c("orig_name" = "prefname"), relationship = "many-to-many", suffix=c("","_orig")) %>%
  left_join(alternate_names %>%
              #filter(!in_panwoodycurr & !in_viewtaxcurr)  
            select(altname, prefname), 
            by=c("wcvp_matched_name" = "prefname"), relationship = "many-to-many", suffix=c("","_wcvpmatch"))


case4_forestgeoalternate <- case4_forestgeoalternate %>% 
  mutate(
    altname = coalesce(
      altname,
      altname_wcvpmatch
    )) %>%   select(-ends_with(c("_orig", "_wcvpmatch")))


# Keep only the ones that have alternate names
case4_forestgeoalternate <- case4_forestgeoalternate %>% filter(!is.na(altname)) %>%
  distinct(orig_name, altname, .keep_all = TRUE) # clearing duplicates

```

Check the different cases:

```{r checkcase4}
# Find the forestgeo alternate name that matches to that alternate name
case4_forestgeoalternate <- case4_forestgeoalternate %>%
  left_join(alternate_names %>%
              filter(in_panwoodycurr | in_viewtaxcurr) %>%
              select(forestgeo_current_name = prefname,
                     altname,  sp6), by = "altname")


datatable(case4_forestgeoalternate %>%
            filter(!is.na(sp6)) %>%
            group_by(orig_name) %>% 
            filter(n() > 1) %>%
            select(sp6, orig_name, altname, forestgeo_current_name, source_list),
          caption="Two or more alternate names for the species match an alternate forestgeo name.")

# keeping distinct orig_name, forestgeo_current_name pairs
# If the forestgeo name is the same as the orig_name also keep that option.
case4_forestgeoalternate <- case4_forestgeoalternate %>%
  distinct(orig_name, forestgeo_current_name, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%# clearing duplicates
  filter(!is.na(sp6)) %>%
  group_by(orig_name) %>% 
  filter(n() == 1 | word(orig_name,2) == word(forestgeo_current_name,2)) %>%
  ungroup()
```

In this case we keep the forestgeo current name if it is the same for both alternate names. For Psychotria hebeclada we keep the one were the species is the same as the orig_name (because it was only a change in genus)

```{r checkcase4_1}
datatable(case4_forestgeoalternate %>%
            filter(!is.na(sp6)) %>%
            group_by(orig_name) %>% 
            filter(n() == 1) %>%
            select(sp6, orig_name, altname, forestgeo_current_name, source_list),
          caption="One alternate name for the species match an alternate forestgeo name.")


case4_forestgeoalternate <- case4_forestgeoalternate %>% 
          filter(!is.na(sp6))  %>%
            group_by(orig_name) %>% 
            filter(n() == 1) %>%
    mutate(current_name = forestgeo_current_name,
         source_current_name = "ForestGeo"
         ) %>%   
  filter(!orig_name %in% matched_species$orig_name &
           !is.na(current_name) & !current_name %in% matched_species$current_name
         )
 
matched_species <- bind_rows(case0_fgsource, case1_directmatch, case2_forestgeoalternate, case3_forestgeoalternate, case4_forestgeoalternate)

bcilist_fgeomatch <- bcilist %>% filter(!orig_name %in% matched_species$orig_name) %>% select(-sp6, -forestgeo_current_name)


```

So far we have the forestgeo names of `r nrow(matched_species)` out of `r nrow(bcilist)`

### Case 5. The binomial of the species matches a binomial in forestgeo current names
```{r binomialcase}
# Create binomial lookup
forestgeo_binomial_lookup <- bind_rows(
  forestgeo_names_lookup,
  panwoody_list %>%
    mutate(matchby = "origbinomial") %>%
    select(match_key = orig_binomial, forestgeo_current_name = orig_name, sp6, sp4, wcvp_accepted_duplicated, matchby),
  panwoody_list %>% 
    mutate(matchby = "wcvpacceptedbinomial") %>% 
    select(match_key = wcvp_accepted_binomial, forestgeo_current_name = orig_name, sp6, sp4, wcvp_accepted_duplicated, matchby)
) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(match_key)) %>%
  # Group and collapse to keep provenance if a key matched in multiple ways
  group_by(match_key, forestgeo_current_name, sp6, sp4, wcvp_accepted_duplicated) %>%
  summarise(
    matchby = paste(matchby, collapse = ", "),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

case5_binomials <- bcilist_fgeomatch %>%
  left_join(
  forestgeo_binomial_lookup, 
  by=c("orig_binomial" = "match_key"), 
  relationship = "many-to-many") %>% 
  filter(!is.na(sp6))

case5_binomials <- case5_binomials %>%
  # Calculate mapping counts for both sides
  group_by(orig_name) %>%
  mutate(n_orig = n()) %>%
  group_by(forestgeo_current_name) %>%
  mutate(n_fg = n()) %>%
  ungroup() 

datatable(case5_binomials %>%
            filter(n_orig > 1) %>%
            select(sp6, orig_name, forestgeo_current_name, source_list),
          caption="Species matched to two or more different forestgeo binomials. This means forestgeo makes a distinction between two subspecies")

# in this case, since both subspecies are different. Keep the one without the subspecies
case5_binomials <- case5_binomials %>% 
  filter(n_orig == 1 | (n_orig > 1 & is.na(word(forestgeo_current_name,3))))
```

In this case we keep the forestgeo name without the subspecies


```{r checkcase5_2}
datatable(case5_binomials %>%
            filter(n_fg > 1) %>%
            select(sp6, orig_name, forestgeo_current_name, source_list),
          caption="The same forestgeo binomial matched to different species name. This means forestgeo does not make a distinction between two subspecies")

case5_binomials <- case5_binomials %>% 
  filter(n_fg == 1 | 
        (n_fg > 1 & word(orig_name,2) == word(orig_name,4)) |
         orig_name == "Clitoria javitensis var. portobellensis"
        )
```

In this case, we keep the forestgeo name if in the original name, the subspecies is the same as the species. And in the case of Clitoria javitensis we keep the forestgeo name for Clitoria javitensis var. portobellensis

```{r checkcase5}
datatable(case5_binomials %>%
            group_by(orig_name,forestgeo_current_name) %>% 
            filter(n() == 1) %>%
            select(sp6, orig_name, forestgeo_current_name, source_list),
          caption="The species binomial found a match in forestgeo")

# Keep the distinct pairs of orig_name, forestgeo name
# If they are not distinct, keep only the ones were subspecies is the same as the species. 

case5_binomials <- case5_binomials %>%
          filter(
            !is.na(sp6))  %>%
            group_by(orig_name, forestgeo_current_name) %>% 
            filter(n() == 1) %>%
    mutate(
      current_name = forestgeo_current_name,
      source_current_name = "ForestGeo"
         ) %>%   
      filter(!orig_name %in% matched_species$orig_name &
           !is.na(current_name) & !current_name %in% matched_species$current_name
         )

matched_species <- bind_rows(case0_fgsource, case1_directmatch, case2_forestgeoalternate, case3_forestgeoalternate, case4_forestgeoalternate, case5_binomials)


```

Now we have the forestgeo names of `r nrow(matched_species)` out of `r nrow(bcilist)`



### Case 6. The species original binomial matches to a forestgeo alternate name

```{r case6_1}

bcilist<- bcilist %>%
  mutate(wcvp_matched_binomial = paste0(word(wcvp_matched_name, 1), " ",word(wcvp_matched_name, 2) ))
    
bcilist_fgeomatch <- bcilist %>% filter(!orig_name %in% matched_species$orig_name) %>% select(-sp6, -forestgeo_current_name)
                                  
                                        
case6_binomial_forestgeoalternate <- bcilist_fgeomatch %>% 
  left_join(alternate_names %>% filter(in_panwoodycurr | in_viewtaxcurr) %>% select(altname, forestgeo_current_name= prefname , sp6), 
            by=c("orig_binomial" = "altname"),suffix = c("", "_orig")) %>%
  left_join(alternate_names %>% filter(in_panwoodycurr | in_viewtaxcurr) %>% select(altname, forestgeo_current_name = prefname , sp6), 
            by=c("wcvp_matched_binomial" = "altname"),suffix = c("", "_wcvpmatch")) 
```



```{r case6_2}

case6_binomial_forestgeoalternate <- case6_binomial_forestgeoalternate %>%  mutate(
    forestgeo_current_name =     coalesce(
      forestgeo_current_name,
      forestgeo_current_name_wcvpmatch
    ),
    sp6 = coalesce(
      sp6,
      sp6_wcvpmatch
    )) %>%   select(-ends_with(c("_orig",  "_prefmatch", "_wcvpmatch"))) 



case6_binomial_forestgeoalternate <- case6_binomial_forestgeoalternate %>%
  mutate(
         current_name = forestgeo_current_name,
         source_current_name = "ForestGeo"
         )%>%
  filter(!is.na(sp6) & 
           !orig_name %in% matched_species$orig_name &
           !is.na(current_name) & !current_name %in% matched_species$current_name
         )


datatable(case6_binomial_forestgeoalternate %>%
          select(sp6, orig_name,forestgeo_current_name, source_list),
          caption="Species binomial name is a forestgeo alternate name",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10),
          )

case6_binomial_forestgeoalternate <- case6_binomial_forestgeoalternate %>% select(-wcvp_matched_binomial)

matched_species <- bind_rows(case0_fgsource, case1_directmatch, case2_forestgeoalternate, case3_forestgeoalternate, case4_forestgeoalternate, case5_binomials, case6_binomial_forestgeoalternate)

bcilist_fgeomatch <- bcilist %>% filter(!orig_name %in% matched_species$orig_name) %>% select(-sp6, -forestgeo_current_name)

```


### Case 7. The species binomial alternate names matches to a forestgeo alternate name

```{r case6fgname}

# Here we have to be careful, a species can have multiple alt names...
case7_binomialalt <- bcilist_fgeomatch %>%
  mutate(wcvp_matched_binomial = paste0(word(wcvp_matched_name, 1), " ", word(wcvp_matched_name, 2) )) %>%
  left_join(alternate_names %>% 
              #filter(!in_panwoodycurr & !in_viewtaxcurr)  
             select(altname, prefname),
```
