```{r case6fgname}
            by=c("orig_binomial" = "prefname"), relationship = "many-to-many", suffix=c("","_orig")) %>%
  left_join(alternate_names %>%
              #filter(!in_panwoodycurr & !in_viewtaxcurr)  
            select(altname, prefname), 
            by=c("wcvp_matched_binomial" = "prefname"), relationship = "many-to-many", suffix=c("","_wcvpmatch"))


case7_binomialalt <- case7_binomialalt %>% 
  mutate(
    altname = coalesce(
      altname,
      altname_wcvpmatch
    )) %>%   select(-ends_with(c("_orig", "_wcvpmatch")))


# Keep only the ones that have alternate names
case7_binomialalt <- case7_binomialalt %>% filter(!is.na(altname)) %>%
  distinct(orig_name, altname, .keep_all = TRUE) # clearing duplicates

```

Check the different cases:

```{r case6binomialas}
# Find the forestgeo alternate name that matches to that alternate name
case7_binomialalt <- case7_binomialalt %>%
  left_join(alternate_names %>%
              filter(in_panwoodycurr | in_viewtaxcurr) %>%
              select(forestgeo_current_name = prefname,
                     altname,  sp6), by = "altname")


datatable(case7_binomialalt %>%
            filter(!is.na(sp6)) %>%
            group_by(orig_name) %>% 
            filter(n() > 1) %>%
            select(sp6, orig_name, altname, forestgeo_current_name, source_list),
          caption="Two or more alternate names for the species match an alternate forestgeo name.")
```

```{r case6binomialas_1}
datatable(case7_binomialalt %>%
            filter(!is.na(sp6)) %>%
            group_by(orig_name) %>% 
            filter(n() == 1) %>%
            select(sp6, orig_name, altname, forestgeo_current_name, source_list),
          caption="One alternate name for the species match an alternate forestgeo name.")


case7_binomialalt <- case7_binomialalt %>% 
          filter(!is.na(sp6))  %>%
            group_by(orig_name) %>% 
            filter(n() == 1) %>%
    mutate(current_name = forestgeo_current_name,
         source_current_name = "ForestGeo"
         ) %>%   
  filter(!orig_name %in% matched_species$orig_name &
           !is.na(current_name) & !current_name %in% matched_species$current_name
         )
 
matched_species <- bind_rows(case0_fgsource, case1_directmatch, case2_forestgeoalternate, case3_forestgeoalternate, case4_forestgeoalternate, case5_binomials, case6_binomial_forestgeoalternate, case7_binomialalt)

bcilist_fgeomatch <- bcilist %>% filter(!orig_name %in% matched_species$orig_name) %>% select(-sp6, -forestgeo_current_name)


```

So far we have the forestgeo names of `r nrow(matched_species)` out of `r nrow(bcilist)`


### Case 8. The species accepted name according to WCVPv13 matches to Forestgeo.

So far we have matched with the original name or the wcvp matched name (to prevent mismatches due to spelling differences). 

Next we try to match the species that have not matched yet by the wcvp_accepted_name. 


```{r case6directwcvp}

case8_directwcvp <- bcilist_fgeomatch %>% 
  left_join(forestgeo_names_lookup, by = c("wcvp_accepted_name"= "match_key"))


case8_directwcvp <- case8_directwcvp %>%
  filter(!is.na(sp6)) %>%
  mutate(forestgeo_current_name = wcvp_accepted_name,
         current_name = forestgeo_current_name,
         source_current_name = "ForestGeo"
         ) %>%   
      filter(!orig_name %in% matched_species$orig_name &
           !is.na(current_name) & !current_name %in% matched_species$current_name
         )

case8_directwcvp %>% group_by(forestgeo_current_name) %>% filter(n() > 1)

datatable(case8_directwcvp %>%
          select(sp6, orig_name, wcvp_matched_name, wcvp_accepted_name, forestgeo_current_name, source_list, matchby),
          caption="Species wcvp accepted name matches a forestgeo name",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10),
          )




matched_species <- bind_rows(case0_fgsource, case1_directmatch, case2_forestgeoalternate, case3_forestgeoalternate, case4_forestgeoalternate, case5_binomials, case6_binomial_forestgeoalternate, case7_binomialalt, case8_directwcvp)


bcilist_fgeomatch <- bcilist %>% filter(!orig_name %in% matched_species$orig_name) %>% select(-sp6, -forestgeo_current_name)

```

Matching by WCVP accepted binomial
```{r case6wcvpbinomial}

case8_binomialwcvp <- bcilist_fgeomatch %>%
  left_join(
  forestgeo_binomial_lookup, 
  by=c("wcvp_accepted_binomial" = "match_key"), 
  relationship = "many-to-many") %>% 
  filter(!is.na(sp6))

datatable(case8_binomialwcvp %>%
          select(sp6, orig_name, wcvp_matched_name, wcvp_accepted_name, forestgeo_current_name, source_list),
          caption="Species original wcvp accepted binomial matches a forestgeo binomial",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10),
          )

case8_binomialwcvp <- case8_binomialwcvp %>%
          filter(
            !is.na(sp6))  %>%
            group_by(orig_name) %>% 
            filter(n() == 1) %>%
            group_by(forestgeo_current_name) %>% 
            filter(n() == 1) %>%
    mutate(
      current_name = forestgeo_current_name,
      source_current_name = "ForestGeo"
         ) %>%   
      filter(!orig_name %in% matched_species$orig_name &
           !is.na(current_name) & !current_name %in% matched_species$current_name
         )
matched_species <- bind_rows(case0_fgsource, case1_directmatch, case2_forestgeoalternate, case3_forestgeoalternate, case4_forestgeoalternate, case5_binomials, case6_binomial_forestgeoalternate, case7_binomialalt, case8_directwcvp, case8_binomialwcvp)

bcilist_fgeomatch <- bcilist %>% filter(!orig_name %in% matched_species$orig_name) %>% select(-sp6, -forestgeo_current_name)
```




## Join everything

```{r joineverything}
 
cols_to_keep <- colnames(bcilist)

# Adding codes and the WCVP columns mission from the Forectgeo current source
cols_to_replace <- c("sp4", "wcvp_accepted_binomial", "wcvp_matched_name", 
                     "wcvp_matched_plant_name_id", "wcvp_matched_ipni_id", 
                     "wcvp_matched_status", "wcvp_accepted_name", 
                     "wcvp_accepted_family", "wcvp_accepted_authority", 
                     "wcvp_accepted_plant_name_id", "wcvp_accepted_powo_id", 
                     "wcvp_accepted_ipni_id", "wcvp_accepted_lifeform")

matched_species <- matched_species %>%
  # Remove existing versions of these columns
  select(-any_of(cols_to_replace)) %>%
  # Join and pull only those columns from the source
  left_join(panwoody_list %>% select(sp6, all_of(cols_to_replace)), by = "sp6")


bcilist_fgnames <- bind_rows(
  matched_species %>% select(all_of(cols_to_keep)),
  bcilist %>%
    filter(!orig_name %in% c(matched_species$orig_name)
           )
)



nrow(bcilist_fgnames)

# check for duplicated current names
datatable(bcilist_fgnames %>%
            filter(!is.na(current_name)) %>%
          group_by(current_name) %>%
          filter(n() > 1) %>%  
          select(sp6,sp4, orig_name,forestgeo_current_name, source_list) %>% arrange(current_name),
          caption="Current name is duplicated",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10),
          )
```


So far we have found the best matches for forestgeo names. If a species is left unmatched to forestgeo it is because it is in no way in the current panama woody list, or because the match created a conflict with other species in the list that had a more direct match to the same forestgeoname (e.g it was a direct match of name while the other matched through a binomial or synonym)

Now for the species we couldnt find a match for, we set the current name as the name they have in their original list. And check again if this could potentially create a conflict with the forestgeo names. In which case the species needs revision.

```{r currentname}

bcilist_fgnames <- bcilist_fgnames %>% 
  mutate(current_name = ifelse(is.na(current_name),
                               orig_name,
                               current_name
                               ),
         source_current_name = ifelse(is.na(source_current_name),
                               source_list,
                               source_current_name
                               ),
         )

datatable(bcilist_fgnames %>%
            filter(!is.na(current_name)) %>%
          group_by(current_name) %>%
          filter(n() > 1) %>%  
          select(sp6,sp4, current_name, orig_name,forestgeo_current_name, source_current_name, source_list ) %>% arrange(current_name),
          caption="Current name is duplicated",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10),
          )

```


### Sanity checks:
```{r}
# Sanity check species that have a forestgeo current name that is not in forestego
bcilist_fgnames %>% filter(source_current_name == "ForestGeo" & !current_name %in% panwoody_list$orig_name)
```

## Add plot presence
Now that we have the Forestgeo current names for all the species in the list, it is easier to match to the plot info. 

```{r addplotinof}

bcilist_fgnames <- bcilist_fgnames %>%
  # 1. Remove the existing columns so there's no conflict
  select(-any_of(c("census_name", "census_plot", "in_census"))) %>%
  # 2. Join the fresh data from census_list
  left_join(
    census_list %>% select(sp6, census_name, census_plot, in_census),
    by = "sp6"
  )

```


## Add woody column
```{r addwoody field}

bcilist_fgnames <- bcilist_fgnames %>%
  mutate(
    # If it has an sp6 code then it is woody or if the binomial is in woody too. 
    woody_forestgeo = !is.na(sp6) |
      orig_binomial %in% forestgeo_binomial_lookup$match_key | 
      wcvp_accepted_binomial %in% forestgeo_binomial_lookup$match_key |
      current_name %in% forestgeo_names_lookup$match_key |
      !is.na(forestgeo_current_name)
  )

colnames(bcilist_fgnames)

# Species in the woody list because they have sp6 code
datatable(bcilist_fgnames %>%
  filter(woody_forestgeo & !is.na(sp6)) %>%
  select(sp6, sp4, current_name, source_current_name, 
         wcvp_accepted_name, 
         wcvp_accepted_lifeform), 
  caption="BCI species that are in the woody list because their name is in Forestgeo",
  options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10))


# Species in the woody list because their binomial is in forestgeo
datatable(bcilist_fgnames %>%
  filter(woody_forestgeo & is.na(sp6)) %>%
  select(current_name, source_current_name, 
         wcvp_accepted_name, 
         wcvp_accepted_lifeform), 
  caption="BCI species that are in the woody list because their binomial is in forestgeo",
  options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10))

bcilist_fgnames <- bcilist_fgnames %>% mutate(garwood_lifeform = LF_RF)



```





# Save BCI Botanist list

In the final excel file, ther ewill be one sheet with the dictionary of the columns, and another with metadata of the source files of this list. 

```{r}

bcilist_fgnames <- bcilist_fgnames %>% 
  mutate(
  garwood_only_BCNM = ifelse(!is.na(chg) & chg == "BCNM", TRUE, FALSE),
) %>% arrange(current_name)

column_descriptions <- list(
  current_name               = "Current name for the species",
  source_current_name        = "Source of the current name",
    
  # BCI plot presence
  sp6                        = "Current Sp6 code of the species in Forestgeo",
  sp4                        = "Current Sp4 code of the species in Forestgeo",
  
  # WCVP matched fields
  wcvp_matched_name          = "Species name as matched in the World Checklist of Vascular Plants (WCVP)",
  wcvp_matched_plant_name_id = "WCVP internal plant name ID for the matched name",
  wcvp_matched_ipni_id       = "IPNI identifier for the matched name in WCVP",
  wcvp_matched_status        = "Taxonomic status of the matched name in WCVP (e.g. accepted, synonym)",
  
  # WCVP accepted fields
  wcvp_accepted_name         = "Currently accepted species name according to WCVP",
  wcvp_accepted_binomial     = "Accepted genus + species binomial according to WCVP",
  wcvp_accepted_family       = "Accepted family according to WCVP",
  wcvp_accepted_authority    = "Taxonomic authority for the accepted name in WCVP",
  wcvp_accepted_plant_name_id = "WCVP internal plant name ID for the accepted name",
  wcvp_accepted_powo_id      = "Plants of the World Online (POWO) identifier for the accepted name",
  wcvp_accepted_ipni_id      = "IPNI identifier for the accepted name in WCVP",
  wcvp_accepted_lifeform     = "Life form classification of the species according to WCVP",
  
  # Source and Garwood
  garwood_name               = "Species name as it appears in Garwood (2009)",
  garwood_synonyms           = "Synonyms listed for this species in Garwood (2009)",
  garwood_lifeform           = "Life form of the species as classified in Garwood (2009)",
  garwood_only_BCNM          = "True if according to Garwood the species is only in BCNM ",
  
  # Zotz
  zotz_name                  = "Species name as it appears in the Zotz epiphyte list",

  # Census plot list
  census_plot                = "Census plot(s) in which this species has been recorded",
  
  
  # Lianas
  lianas_name                = "Species name as it appears in the lianas",
  

  woody_forestgeo            = "Whether the species is classified as woody according to presence in Forestgeo or binomial presence in Forestgeo"
)

dict_columns <- data.frame(
  column      = names(column_descriptions),
  description = unlist(column_descriptions),
  row.names   = NULL
)


# Metadata sheet
metadata_df <- data.frame(
  item        = c(
    "Report",
    "Date created",
    "Garwood list (+ WCVP v13)",
    "Garwood synonyms",
    "Zotz epiphytes list (+ WCVP v13)",
    "Lianas list (+ WCVP v13)",
    "Panama Biota list (+ WCVP v13)",
    "Panama woody species list (+ WCVP v13)",
    "Panama woody synonyms",
    "Alternate names",
    "Alternate names (Garwood subset)"
  ),
  description = c(
    "A detailed report of the creation of this list can be found at: https://github.com/PanamaForestGEO/Panama_plant_species_lists/tree/master/reports",
    format(Sys.Date(), "%Y-%m-%d"),
    FILE_GARWOOD,
    FILE_GARWOOD_SYN,
    FILE_ZOTZ,
    FILE_LIANAS,
    FILE_PBIOTA,
    FILE_PANWOODY,
    FILE_PANWOODY_SYN,
    FILE_ALTNAMES,
    FILE_ALTNAMES_GARWOOD
  )
)

write_xlsx(
  list(
    "BCI List"   = bcilist_fgnames %>% select(all_of(dict_columns$column)),
    "Dictionary" = dict_columns,
    "Metadata"   = metadata_df
  ),
  FILECOMBINEDLIST
)

datatable(bcilist_fgnames %>% 
             select(all_of(dict_columns$column)), 
  caption="BCI BOTANISTS SPECIES LIST",
  options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10))



knitr::kable(
  bind_rows(
    # non-NA count for presence/absence fields
    bcilist_fgnames %>%
      summarise(across(c(sp6, sp4, garwood_name, census_plot, zotz_name, lianas_name),
                       ~ sum(!is.na(.)))) %>%
      pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "field", values_to = "n") %>%
      mutate(description = "not NA"),
    
    # TRUE/FALSE counts for woody
    bcilist_fgnames %>%
      summarise(woody_TRUE  = sum(woody_forestgeo == TRUE,  na.rm = TRUE),
                woody_FALSE = sum(woody_forestgeo == FALSE, na.rm = TRUE),
                woody_NA    = sum(is.na(woody_forestgeo))) %>%
      pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "field", values_to = "n") %>%
      mutate(description = c("woody = TRUE", "woody = FALSE", "woody = NA"))
  ) %>%
  mutate(total = nrow(bcilist_fgnames),
         pct   = round(100 * n / total, 1)),
  
  caption = "Summary of botanists list"
)

```


```{r posibblywoody}
wcvp_lifeform_woody_list = c(
  "tree",
  "shrub",
  "liana",
  "climbing shrub",
  "shrub or tree",
  "climbing shrub or tree",
  "scrambling shrub or tree",
  "subshrub or shrub",
  "subshrub, shrub or tree",
  "scrambling shrub",
  "perennial or shrub",
  "scrambling subshrub or shrub",
  "epiphytic shrub",
  "scrambling tree",
  "climbing shrub or liana",
  "scrambling shrub or liana",
  "tuberous shrub or tree",
  "herbaceous tree",
  "climbing herbaceous tree",
  "climbing herbaceous tree or liana",
  "climbing perennial or herbaceous tree",
  "subshrub or climbing shrub",
  "tuberous subshrub",
  "hemiparasitic shrub",
  "hemiepiphytic shrub or tree",
  "annual or shrub",
  "annual, subshrub or shrub"
)

# List of target abbreviations for filtering
#L Liana (to canopy)
#M Medium-sized tree: 10-30 m
#S Shrub: 1-5 m
#T Tree: >30 m
#U Treelet: 5-10 m
  
  
target_codes <- "[LMSTU]"
garwoodlf <- unique(bcilist$garwood_lifeform)
garwood_woody_lifeforms <- garwoodlf[grepl(target_codes, garwoodlf) & !grepl("a", garwoodlf, ignore.case = TRUE)]

print(garwood_woody_lifeforms)



bcilist_possibly_woody <- bcilist_fgnames %>%
  filter(!woody_forestgeo & is.na(`BCI cult`) &
          (wcvp_accepted_lifeform %in% wcvp_lifeform_woody_list |
           garwood_lifeform %in% garwood_woody_lifeforms 
             )  ) 

datatable(bcilist_possibly_woody %>%
  select(orig_name, wcvp_matched_name, wcvp_accepted_name, wcvp_accepted_family, 
         wcvp_accepted_lifeform, garwood_lifeform), 
          caption="BCI species that might be woody according to the wcvp lifeform",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10))

# Save list
write_xlsx(
  list(
    "BCI possibly woody"   = bcilist_possibly_woody %>% select(all_of(dict_columns$column)),
    "Dictionary" = dict_columns,
    "Metadata"   = metadata_df
  ),
  FILEBCIWOODY
)


```


# Wright revision: Using the BCI Botanist list to find Wright species that need revision.

Here we check what Wright species are not in the panama woody_forestgeo list but might be woody_forestgeo.

```{r sp4missing}

# Lookup table for woody_forestgeo species
woody_names_lookup <- bind_rows(
  panwoody_list %>% mutate(matchby = "Fcurr") %>% 
    select(match_key = orig_name, forestgeo_current_name = orig_name, sp6, sp4, wcvp_accepted_duplicated, matchby),
  
  panwoody_list %>% mutate(matchby = "wcvpmatch") %>% 
    select(match_key = wcvp_matched_name, forestgeo_current_name = orig_name, sp6, sp4, wcvp_accepted_duplicated, matchby),
  
    panwoody_list %>% mutate(matchby = "wcvpaccepted") %>% 
    select(match_key = wcvp_accepted_name, forestgeo_current_name = orig_name, sp6, sp4, wcvp_accepted_duplicated, matchby),
  
    panwoody_list %>%
    mutate(matchby = "origbinomial") %>%
    select(match_key = orig_binomial, forestgeo_current_name = orig_name, sp6, sp4, wcvp_accepted_duplicated, matchby),
  
  panwoody_list %>% 
    mutate(matchby = "wcvpacceptedbinomial") %>% 
    select(match_key = wcvp_accepted_binomial, forestgeo_current_name = orig_name, sp6, sp4, wcvp_accepted_duplicated, matchby),
  
  panwoody_synonyms %>% 
    mutate(matchby = "altname") %>% 
    select(match_key = altname, forestgeo_current_name , matchby)
) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(match_key)) %>%
  # Group and collapse to keep provenance if a key matched in multiple ways
  group_by(match_key, forestgeo_current_name, sp6, sp4, wcvp_accepted_duplicated) %>%
  summarise(
    matchby = paste(matchby, collapse = ", "),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Look up table for BCI species
bcilist_lookup <- bind_rows(
  # 1. Match by Original Name
  bcilist_fgnames %>% 
    mutate(matchby = "orig_name") %>% 
    select(match_key = orig_name, sp6, sp4, woody_forestgeo, matchby),
  
  # 2. Match by Original Binomial
  bcilist_fgnames %>% 
    mutate(matchby = "orig_binomial") %>% 
    select(match_key = orig_binomial, sp6, sp4, woody_forestgeo, matchby),
  
  # 3. Match by WCVP Matched Name
  bcilist_fgnames %>% 
    mutate(matchby = "wcvpmatch") %>% 
    select(match_key = wcvp_matched_name, sp6, sp4, woody_forestgeo, matchby),
  
  # 4. Match by WCVP Accepted Name
  bcilist_fgnames %>% 
    mutate(matchby = "wcvpaccepted") %>% 
    select(match_key = wcvp_accepted_name, sp6, sp4, woody_forestgeo, matchby),
  
  # 5. Match by WCVP Accepted Binomial
  bcilist_fgnames %>% 
    mutate(matchby = "wcvpacceptedbinomial") %>% 
    select(match_key = wcvp_accepted_binomial, sp6, sp4, woody_forestgeo, matchby),
    
  # 6. Match by Current Name (from your specific BCI columns)
  bcilist_fgnames %>% 
    mutate(matchby = "current_name") %>% 
    select(match_key = current_name, sp6, sp4, woody_forestgeo, matchby)
) %>% 
  # Drop NAs and apply your regex cleaning from before
  filter(!is.na(match_key)) %>%
  # Group and collapse to keep provenance
  group_by(match_key, sp6, sp4, woody_forestgeo) %>%
  summarise(
    matchby = paste(unique(matchby), collapse = ", "),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

bcilist_lookup_woody <- bcilist_lookup %>% filter(woody_forestgeo)

wright_no_woody_match <- allwrighttaxa %>%
  mutate(
    # field if there is no match at all with the woody_forestgeo list
    no_panwoody_match = !(
      sp6 %in% na.omit(woody_names_lookup$sp6) |
      sp4 %in% na.omit(woody_names_lookup$sp4) |
      orig_name %in% woody_names_lookup$match_key |
      orig_binomial %in% woody_names_lookup$match_key |
      wcvp_accepted_name %in% woody_names_lookup$match_key |
      wcvp_matched_name %in% woody_names_lookup$match_key 
    ),

    no_bci_woody_match <- !(
      sp6 %in% na.omit(bcilist_lookup_woody$sp6) |
      sp4 %in% na.omit(bcilist_lookup_woody$sp4) |
      orig_name %in% bcilist_lookup_woody$match_key |
      orig_binomial %in% bcilist_lookup_woody$match_key |
      wcvp_accepted_name %in% bcilist_lookup_woody$match_key |
      wcvp_matched_name %in% bcilist_lookup_woody$match_key 
    ),
      
    lifeform_wright_is_woody = str_detect(lifeform_wright, "TREE|MIDSTORY|UNDERSTORY|SHRUB|LIANA"),
    congeners_woody = (word(orig_binomial,1) %in% word(woody_names_lookup$match_key,1)),
    climber = str_detect(lifeform_wright, "CLIMBER"),

    # check first by s6 code
    lifeform_garwood = bcilist_fgnames$garwood_lifeform[match(allwrighttaxa$sp6,bcilist_fgnames$sp6)],
    # if still NA check by sp4 code
    lifeform_garwood = ifelse(
             is.na(lifeform_garwood),
             bcilist_fgnames$garwood_lifeform[match(allwrighttaxa$sp4,bcilist_fgnames$sp4)],
             lifeform_garwood
            ),
    
    lifeform_garwood_is_woody =  lifeform_garwood %in% garwood_woody_lifeforms,
    
    lifeform_wcvp_is_woody = wcvp_lifeform_description %in% wcvp_lifeform_woody_list,
    
    might_be_woody = lifeform_wright_is_woody | congeners_woody | lifeform_garwood_is_woody | lifeform_wcvp_is_woody
           )

# Table of Wright unmatched species
datatable(wright_no_woody_match %>% 
            filter(no_panwoody_match & Idlevel != "unidentified") %>%
            select(sp4, sp6, orig_name, orig_name,
                   might_be_woody,          lifeform_wright_is_woody,
         lifeform_wcvp_is_woody,
         lifeform_garwood_is_woody, congeners_woody,
                   lifeform_wright, Idlevel, lifeform_garwood, wcvp_lifeform_description
                   ) %>%
            arrange(desc(might_be_woody), orig_name),
          caption = "Wright morphospecies missing from the woody_forestgeo list. The column might_be_woody indicates if it could be woody_forestgeo based on the wright lifeform",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10))


orig_cols <- colnames(wright_no_woody_match)

wright_no_woody_match <- wright_no_woody_match %>% 
  relocate(might_be_woody,
         lifeform_wright_is_woody,
         lifeform_wcvp_is_woody,
         lifeform_garwood_is_woody,
         congeners_woody,
         Idlevel,
         sp6, sp4, 
         orig_name, 
         wcvp_matched_name, wcvp_accepted_name, wcvp_matched_status,
         orig_family, wcvp_accepted_family, 
         might_be_woody, oldname,
         orig_author, wcvp_accepted_authority,
         lifeform_wright,
         lifeform_garwood,
         wcvp_lifeform_description,
         climber
         ) %>%
  arrange(desc(might_be_woody), Idlevel, orig_name)

write_xlsx(wright_no_woody_match, FILECHECKWRIGHT)

```

# Panama biota revision
this needs revision...
```{r panamabiotarevision}
pbiota_list$wcvp_accepted_binomial <- paste0(
  pbiota_list$wcvp_accepted_name_genus, " ",
  pbiota_list$wcvp_accepted_name_species
)

# 1. direct name/binomial match
pbiota_garwood_name_match <- pbiota_list %>%
  filter(

    orig_name %in% bcilist_fgnames$orig_name |

    (!is.na(wcvp_matched_name) &
       wcvp_matched_name %in% bcilist_fgnames$wcvp_matched_name) |

    (!is.na(wcvp_accepted_name) &
       wcvp_accepted_name %in% bcilist_fgnames$wcvp_accepted_name) |

    (!is.na(orig_binomial) & (
        orig_binomial %in% bcilist_fgnames$orig_binomial 
    )) |

    (!is.na(wcvp_accepted_binomial) & (
        wcvp_accepted_binomial %in% bcilist_fgnames$wcvp_accepted_binomial 
    )) |
      
    (!is.na(orig_name) & (
        orig_name %in% bcilist_fgnames$current_name 
    ))
  )

# 2. synonym_sp for remaining
pbiota_garwood_synonym_match <- pbiota_list %>%
  filter(
    !orig_name %in% pbiota_garwood_name_match$orig_name &
    (
      orig_name %in% garwood_synonyms$synonym_sp |
      (!is.na(wcvp_matched_name) &
         wcvp_matched_name %in% garwood_synonyms$synonym_sp) |
      (!is.na(wcvp_accepted_name) &
         wcvp_accepted_name %in% garwood_synonyms$synonym_sp) |
      (!is.na(orig_binomial) & orig_binomial %in% garwood_synonyms$synonym_binomial) |
      (!is.na(wcvp_accepted_binomial) & wcvp_accepted_binomial %in% garwood_synonyms$synonym_binomial)
    )
  )

pbiota_garwood_synonym_match$matched_garwood_synonym <- TRUE


# see species that matched by a synonym 
pbiota_garwood_synonym_match_print <- pbiota_garwood_synonym_match %>%  left_join(
    garwood_synonyms %>% select(sp_orig = sp_name, synonym_sp, syn_orig = SynonymTextALL) %>% distinct(),
    by = c("orig_name" = "synonym_sp")
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    garwood_synonyms %>% select(sp_match = sp_name, synonym_sp, syn_match = SynonymTextALL) %>% distinct(),
    by = c("wcvp_matched_name" = "synonym_sp"), relationship = "many-to-many"
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    garwood_synonyms %>% select(sp_wcvp = sp_name, synonym_sp, syn_wcvp = SynonymTextALL) %>% distinct(),
    by = c("wcvp_accepted_name" = "synonym_sp"),  relationship = "many-to-many") %>%
  mutate(
    SynonymMatchAll = coalesce(syn_orig, syn_match, syn_wcvp),
    orig_garwood = coalesce(sp_orig, sp_match, sp_wcvp),
  ) %>%
  select(-syn_orig, -syn_match, -syn_wcvp,  -sp_orig, -sp_match, -sp_wcvp) %>%
  distinct(orig_name, .keep_all = TRUE)
  

datatable(pbiota_garwood_synonym_match_print %>% 
            select(orig_name, wcvp_matched_name, wcvp_accepted_name, SynonymMatchAll, orig_garwood), 
          caption="Panamabiota species that matched Garwood by synonym",
          options = list(scrollX = TRUE, pageLength = 10))
```
